blurbs.blurb: Primarch of the Ultramarines and author of the Codex Astartes; resurrected in the 41st Millennium, he now leads the Imperium as Lord Commander after ten thousand years.
Primarch of the Thousand Sons, a peerless psyker whose pursuit of sorcery led his Legion to ruin and into the service of the Chaos God Tzeentch.
Primarch of the Iron Hands, slain by his fallen brother Fulgrim during the Dropsite Massacre on Istvaan V.
Primarch of the Salamanders, a renowned smith and Perpetual known for his compassion and his devotion to protecting humanity.
Primarch of the Death Guard, who turned traitor during the Horus Heresy and became a daemon primarch of the Plague God Nurgle.
Primarch of the Sons of Horus and Warmaster of the Imperium, whose corruption by Chaos sparked the galaxy-spanning civil war known as the Horus Heresy.
Primarch of the Dark Angels, recently returned after millennia lost in the warp; his ancient feud with his lieutenant Luther created the Fallen.
Primarch of the Space Wolves, a ferocious warrior who departed on a mysterious quest and is prophesied to return at the Imperium's hour of greatest need.
Primarch of the Word Bearers, the first primarch to embrace Chaos worship, who set the Horus Heresy in motion and became a daemon primarch.
Primarch of the Emperor's Children, whose quest for perfection led him to fall to the Chaos God Slaanesh and become a daemon primarch.
The last-discovered primarch and twin of Omegon, who together led the Alpha Legion; master of infiltration, his death and identity were deliberately obscured.
Twin brother of Alpharius and co-commander of the secretive Alpha Legion, whose very existence was concealed even within the Legion.
Primarch of the World Eaters, enslaved by neural implants that drove him to berserk rage; he fell to the Blood God Khorne and became a daemon primarch.
Primarch of the Imperial Fists and master of siegecraft, who oversaw the defence of the Imperial Palace during the Siege of Terra.
Primarch of the Raven Guard, a master of stealth warfare who vanished into the Eye of Terror after the Heresy in search of atonement.
Primarch of the Night Lords, a tormented figure plagued by violent visions who ruled through terror and was eventually assassinated at his own invitation.
Primarch of the Blood Angels, a winged and beloved figure who died defending Terra in single combat against the Warmaster Horus.
Primarch of the White Scars, a master of swift cavalry-style warfare from the plains world of Chogoris.
Primarch of the Iron Warriors, a brilliant and bitter siege-master who turned traitor and became a daemon primarch of Chaos.
The immortal founder and ruler of the Imperium of Mankind, a being of vast psychic power who has sat entombed on the Golden Throne of Terra since the Horus Heresy.
A Colonel-Commissar of the Astra Militarum who leads the Tanith First-and-Only; the protagonist of the Gaunt's Ghosts novels set in the Sabbat Worlds Crusade.
An Inquisitor of the Ordo Xenos and protagonist of the Eisenhorn novels, whose career charts a gradual slide from puritan orthodoxy toward radicalism.
An Inquisitor and former pupil of Eisenhorn, paralysed and confined to a life-support chair, who leads his own retinue across the Ravenor novels.
A celebrated Commissar of the Astra Militarum and self-styled Hero of the Imperium, whose comedic memoirs recount a heroic reputation he insists is undeserved.
A champion of the World Eaters and the Blood God Khorne, infamous for slaughtering friend and foe alike in unrelenting battle-frenzy.
A Captain of the Ultramarines and protagonist of Graham McNeill's Ultramarines novels, known for bending the strictures of the Codex Astartes.
A Wolf Lord of the Space Wolves, raised from a Fenrisian youth to one of the Chapter's most celebrated heroes; protagonist of the Space Wolf novels.
Captain of the Ultramarines Second Company, a decorated and ambitious officer renowned for both battlefield brilliance and pride.
Chapter Master of the Ultramarines and Lord of Macragge, one of the most distinguished commanders in the Imperium.
Chief Librarian of the Blood Angels, called the Lord of Death for mastering the Black Rage that consumes his Chapter's psyche.
Supreme Overlord of the Dark Eldar city of Commorragh, a ruthless and ancient schemer who rose from low birth to rule the Dark City.
The most renowned Farseer of Craftworld Ulthwe, an ancient Aeldari psyker who manipulates events across the galaxy to safeguard his people.
An enigmatic member of the Fallen Dark Angels, the Lord of the Fallen, who roams the Imperium toward Terra for purposes unknown.
Herald of Nurgle and host of the Destroyer Hive, a lord of the Death Guard who once served as Mortarion's First Captain under the name Calas Typhon.
The Silent King, last overlord of the Necrons, who led their race in the war against the C'tan and now returns to unite the dynasties against the galaxy's threats.
An Inquisitor of the Ordo Xenos who compiles and annotates the memoirs of Commissar Ciaphas Cain, with whom she is romantically entangled.
A renegade Apothecary of the Emperor's Children, an amoral master of genetic and flesh-craft notorious for his monstrous creations.
A Warsmith of the Iron Warriors, a half-breed risen to command who becomes a recurring antagonist across Graham McNeill's Ultramarines novels.
The greatest of the Dark Angels' followers and Lion El'Jonson's mentor, whose fall to Chaos created the Fallen and doomed the world of Caliban.

Major and one of the Tanith First's founding officers, Rawne is Gaunt's most dangerous subordinate — capable, ruthless, and long harbouring a personal hatred for his colonel-commissar. Despite that enmity, his battlefield competence makes him indispensable.

The Tanith First's foremost marksman, Larkin is a long-las specialist whose uncanny accuracy borders on preternatural. Volatile and eccentric — earning him the nickname 'Mad' — his mental instability in no way diminishes his value as the regiment's deadliest sniper.

Chief Scout of the Tanith First-and-Only, Mkoll is regarded as one of the finest reconnaissance specialists in the Sabbat Worlds Crusade. Silent, self-contained, and supernaturally gifted in fieldcraft, he is the standard against which all Ghost scouts are measured.

Commissar assigned to the Tanith First-and-Only, Hark initially arrives as an adversarial watchdog but over time becomes a steadying force within the regiment. A former Navy officer, he brings an unusual breadth of experience to his duties.

Aide and dogsbody to Commissar Ciaphas Cain, Jurgen is unassuming in manner but possesses a rare psychic blank quality — his null aura disrupts psykers and daemons alike. Malodorous and methodical, he has saved Cain's life on more occasions than either likely cares to count.

A former Vervunhive ganger who joined the Tanith First-and-Only after the Chaos siege of Verghast, Criid rose to become the regiment's first female sergeant and eventual commander of A Company. Her grit and street-honed instincts forged her into one of the Ghosts' most dependable leaders.

Colonel and second-in-command of the Tanith First-and-Only, Corbec is the regiment's warm, boisterous heart — a beloved figure who balances Gaunt's discipline with a father's approachability. A native Tanith, he carries the weight of his lost world with quiet dignity.

Trooper Dermon Caffran is one of the Tanith First's original Tanith-born soldiers — young, earnest, and a steady presence in the ranks. His relationship with Tona Criid and their adoption of two orphaned children give him an unusually domestic anchor amid the grind of the crusade.

A former Vervun Primary PDF officer, Captain Ban Daur joined the Tanith First-and-Only after the siege of Verghast and became a key liaison between the regiment's native Tanith core and its large Verghastite contingent. Reliable and personable, he excels in support and command roles.

Colonel and commanding officer of the Valhallan 597th, Kasteen is Ciaphas Cain's direct superior and a capable, pragmatic commander. She rebuilt the regiment's cohesion after a damaging internal schism, and her steady professionalism provides the institutional backbone Cain so conspicuously avoids.

Colonel of the Valhallan 597th Ice Warriors and standing executive officer of the regiment under Commissar Ciaphas Cain. A steady, pragmatic commander, Broklaw serves as a reliable foil to Cain's reluctant heroics throughout the Cain novels.

Senior medicae officer attached to the Tanith First-and-Only in Dan Abnett's Gaunt's Ghosts series. Curth is one of the regiment's most enduring non-combat figures, providing medical care and a civilian moral perspective across many campaigns.

A powerful daemonhost — a human body forcibly inhabited by a warp entity — bound into service by Inquisitor Eisenhorn. Cherubael is a recurring and dangerous tool in the Eisenhorn trilogy, emblematic of the moral compromises that define Eisenhorn's descent toward radicalism.

A senior officer of the archenemy warband the Sons of Sek who defects and becomes a vital intelligence asset for the Sabbat Worlds Crusade. His insider knowledge of archenemy forces drives much of the plot in Abnett's later Gaunt's Ghosts novels.

An untouchable — a psychic blank — first recruited by Inquisitor Eisenhorn as an agent and operative. Bequin is a central figure across both the Eisenhorn trilogy and the subsequent Bequin trilogy, where she leads her own Inquisitorial cell.

A veteran bounty hunter and close-combat specialist who serves as a trusted operative in Inquisitor Eisenhorn's warband and later Ravenor's. Known for his toughness and loyalty, Nayl is a recurring action-focused presence across both the Eisenhorn and Ravenor novel series.

A telekinetic psyker and covert operative in Inquisitor Ravenor's warband. Kys is one of the warband's primary field agents, using her psychic abilities in close-quarters operations throughout the Ravenor trilogy by Dan Abnett.

A savant and scholar in the service of Inquisitor Eisenhorn, augmented with extensive data-storage implants. Aemos functions as Eisenhorn's encyclopaedic reference and advisor, accompanying him through much of the Eisenhorn trilogy.

An Adeptus Arbites officer who becomes one of Inquisitor Eisenhorn's earliest field companions. Fischig represents a more orthodox Imperial perspective within the warband, and his relationship with Eisenhorn grows strained as the Inquisitor makes increasingly radical choices.

A corrupt noble and Chaos-aligned antagonist in the Eisenhorn trilogy, reduced to a disembodied brain preserved in a life-support casket after his original body is destroyed. Despite his helpless form, Glaw remains manipulative and dangerous, reappearing across multiple Eisenhorn novels.

A skilled acrobat and covert operative who serves in both Eisenhorn's and later Ravenor's warbands. Swole's agility and infiltration skills make her a valuable field agent throughout the Eisenhorn and Ravenor novel series by Dan Abnett.

An Interrogator assigned to Inquisitor Ravenor's warband in Dan Abnett's Ravenor trilogy. Thonius is an ambitious and intellectually driven agent whose loyalties and judgement are tested severely over the course of the series.

An untouchable — a psychic null — employed by Inquisitor Eisenhorn and later Ravenor as a limiter and counter-psyker asset. Frauka is a morally ambiguous figure, tolerated for his utility despite his unsettling blankness and personal vices.

A young Tanith native who joins the First-and-Only as a piper and informal mascot in Dan Abnett's Gaunt's Ghosts series. Milo is notable for his seemingly prophetic instincts and his close bond with Colonel-Commissar Gaunt from the earliest novels.

Chief medical officer of the Tanith First-and-Only throughout Dan Abnett's Gaunt's Ghosts series. A civilian doctor pressed into service when Tanith was destroyed, Dorden is defined by his pacifist ethic and quiet moral courage over decades of campaigning.

Try Again Bragg was the gentle giant of the Tanith First-and-Only, famed as the regiment's heavy weapons specialist. Despite his imposing size, he was known for a mild temperament and steadfast loyalty to his comrades.

Felyx Chass served with the Verghastite intake of the Tanith First-and-Only. The character's arc is tied closely to questions of identity and lineage within the regiment's later campaigns on the Sabbat Worlds front.

Pasanius Lysane was a sergeant of the Ultramarines and the closest comrade of Captain Uriel Ventris, accompanying him through exile and the events of the Nightbringer and Warriors of Ultramar novels. His loyalty to Ventris never wavered, even when both were cast out of the Chapter.

Zygmunt Molotch was a renegade psyker and recurring antagonist in Dan Abnett's Ravenor trilogy, serving Chaos as a highly intelligent and ruthless operative. He proved one of Inquisitor Ravenor's most dangerous and persistent enemies across multiple novels.

Sanian was a Hagian esholi — a local scholar — encountered by the Tanith First on the shrine world of Hagia. Initially a guide for the Ghosts, she became spiritually significant when the soul of Saint Sabbat chose her as its vessel during the events of 'Sabbat Martyr'.

Lord General Lugo was the Imperial Guard commander overseeing operations on Herodor during the Sabbat Worlds Crusade. His fractious relationship with Gaunt and his politically motivated decisions placed him at odds with the field officers around him.

Gerome Landerson was a leader within the Gereon underground resistance during the Chaos occupation of that world. He guided Gaunt's covert insertion team — the Gereon Twelve — across the planet in the 'Traitor General' novel, helping them locate and extract a high-value Imperial defector.

Dalin Criid was raised as the adopted son of Tanith trooper Tona Criid, having survived the Vervunhive siege as a child. He joined the Tanith First-and-Only as a Guardsman when he came of age, though his true nature as one of Heritor Asphodel's woe machines was revealed in the series' later novels.

Ario Barzano was an Inquisitor of the Ordo Xenos who worked undercover during the campaign for Fortis Binary in Dan Abnett's 'First and Only'. Posing as a political officer attached to Gaunt's regiment, he pursued a clandestine investigation into a Chaos artefact known as the Vermilion Level secret.

Midas Betancore was a skilled pilot and valued operative in Inquisitor Gregor Eisenhorn's retinue, appearing across the early Eisenhorn novels. His background as an expert flyer made him indispensable to the team's covert operations.

Zael Efferneti was a young hive-city street kid and latent psyker encountered by Inquisitor Ravenor on Eustis Majoris, where addiction to the warp-tainted drug Flects had unlocked strange abilities in him. He was later handed to the Inquisition and eventually became the Grey Knight known as Hyperion.

Beta Bequin is a pariah — a blank with no presence in the warp — who becomes central to the Eisenhorn and Bequin novel series bearing her name. She was raised among the Cognitate, an organisation of untouchables, and her unique nature makes her both a weapon and a target in the war between Eisenhorn and Chaos.

Lord General Hechtor Dravere was an Imperial Guard commander and primary antagonist in Dan Abnett's 'Necropolis'-era Gaunt's Ghosts narrative, driven by personal ambition and willingness to expend lives for political gain. His scheme to seize a powerful Chaos artefact brought him into direct conflict with Gaunt.

Agun Soric was a veteran sergeant in the Verghastite intake of the Tanith First-and-Only, respected for his steadiness and leadership in the field. He harboured a concealed psychic ability — communicating via messages that appeared spontaneously on scraps of paper — which eventually forced a crisis around his fate within the regiment.

Gol Kolea was a Verghastite miner who survived the Zoican siege to become one of the Tanith First-and-Only's most dependable sergeants. His personal story is haunted by the loss of his family and the later revelation that the children he believed had survived were woe machines planted by Heritor Asphodel.

Chaos Magister and self-styled warlord of the Zoican Host during the Sabbat Worlds Crusade. Asphodel corrupted the entire population of Ferrozoica Hive on Verghast and led millions against rival Vervunhive in a total-war siege. He was slain by Gaunt aboard his armoured crawler headquarters.

Commissar attached to the Tanith First-and-Only, and a long-standing personal acquaintance of Ibram Gaunt from their shared years at Schola Progenium. Though outwardly genial and fond of creature comforts, Blenner is a morally compromised figure whose loyalties are tested repeatedly across the later Sabbat Worlds campaigns.

Trooper of the Tanith First-and-Only, notorious within the regiment as a cold-blooded and deeply dangerous soldier. Cuu's brutal nature and total lack of moral scruple made him one of the most unsettling figures in the Ghosts' ranks, and his actions against fellow soldiers mark him as a villain within his own unit.

Beati of the Imperial Ecclesiarchy and namesake of the Sabbat Worlds Crusade. A holy warrior canonised for driving Chaos from the Sabbat Worlds in ancient times, she was reborn in mortal flesh during the Crusade to rally Imperial forces at their darkest hour and personally slew the Chaos Magister Enok Innokenti.

Chaos Magister serving under Archon Nadzybar during the Sabbat Worlds Crusade, and commander of the assault on the holy city of Civitas Beati. His primary objective was to hunt down and destroy the reborn Saint Sabbat; he was ultimately decapitated by her in personal combat, an act that broke the morale of his besieging forces.

Sergeant in the Tanith First-and-Only and longtime aide to Major Rawne. Feygor is a gruff, pragmatic soldier whose close association with the regiment's most ruthless officers shaped his role in the unit. He serves in numerous campaigns across the Sabbat Worlds and is among the core supporting cast of the Ghosts.

Trooper and designated flamer-operator of the Tanith First-and-Only. Brostin has a well-documented enthusiasm for fire and incendiary work that borders on the obsessive, making him the regiment's go-to specialist for close-assault flame duties across many campaigns of the Sabbat Worlds Crusade.

Scout and specialist soldier of the Tanith First-and-Only, regarded as one of the most exceptional infiltrators in the regiment. Mkvenner possesses an almost preternatural talent for stealth and close combat, and his origins on Tanith gave him skills that consistently placed him at the forefront of the Ghosts' covert operations.

Vox-operator and trooper of the Tanith First-and-Only. Beltayn serves as a reliable communications specialist within the regiment, maintaining contact between units during the complex operations that define the Ghosts' campaigns across the Sabbat Worlds.

Scout-trooper of the Tanith First-and-Only, serving as one of the regiment's forward reconnaissance specialists. Bonin operates closely with Mkvenner and other scouts, and his fieldcraft and situational awareness made him a dependable asset across the Ghosts' many campaigns.

Sergeant of the Tanith First-and-Only, recognisable for his augmetic shoulder that replaced a limb lost in combat. A capable and dependable non-commissioned officer, Varl is one of the longer-serving Ghost sergeants and a steady presence through many of the regiment's most gruelling engagements.

Imperial Guard Lord-General of the Volpone regiments and eventual traitor to the Imperium. After repeated acts of cowardice and malfeasance — including ordering an artillery strike on Gaunt's own troops — Sturm was stripped of rank, mind-locked, and taken for trial, only to be captured by Chaos forces and exploited for the strategic intelligence locked in his memory.

Warmaster of the Sabbat Worlds Crusade and supreme Imperial commander following the death of his predecessor Slaydo. Macaroth directed the decades-long military campaign to retake the Sabbat Worlds from Chaos, though his strategic judgment and personal conduct were subjects of ongoing tension among his senior officers.

Chaos warlord bearing the title Anarch and founder-commander of the Sons of Sek, an elite Chaos force deliberately modelled on the Imperial Guard. A senior Magister of the Sabbat Worlds Archonate, Sek is the primary antagonist of the later Sabbat Worlds novels and a rival in ambition to Archon Gaur himself.

Captain of the Ultramarines Fourth Company and direct predecessor of Uriel Ventris. Idaeus was noted for a willingness to deviate from strict Codex doctrine when circumstances demanded, a trait he passed on to his successor. He died on Thracia destroying a strategically vital bridge, entrusting his power sword and command to Ventris in his final moments.

The Nightbringer is one of the ancient C'tan, star-gods predating the galaxy's mortal races. Imprisoned in a fragmented shard form, it was sealed beneath the planet Pavonis and became the focal threat of the first Uriel Ventris novel.

Astador is a Chaplain of the Mortifactors, an Ultramarines successor Chapter known for its death-cult practices. He led a Mortifactors contingent to Tarsis Ultra to fight alongside Uriel Ventris's Fourth Company against a Tyranid invasion.

Ardaric Vaanes was once a Shadow Captain of the Raven Guard before turning renegade and joining the Red Corsairs. He later served under Warsmith Honsou, though his loyalty ultimately wavered at a critical moment during the invasion of Ultramar.

Mykola Shonai served as planetary governor of Pavonis and is a central figure in Graham McNeill's novel Nightbringer. Her secret dealings with a Tau delegation in pursuit of greater autonomy from the Imperium set the political conflict of that story in motion.

Leto Barbaden was an Imperial Guard officer who became governor of the world of Salinas after engineering a massacre of the city of Khaturian to crush an insurgency. He serves as the principal antagonist of Graham McNeill's The Killing Ground.

Daron Nisato was a former Administratum official on Salinas who became a reluctant fighter with the Sons of Salinas resistance movement. He appears in Graham McNeill's The Killing Ground, taking up leadership of the insurgents following Sylvanus Thayer.

Sylvanus Thayer was the charismatic founding leader of the Sons of Salinas, an underground resistance on the world of Salinas. He features in Graham McNeill's The Killing Ground as the insurgent whose defiance provoked Governor Barbaden's infamous massacre.

Shas'O Or'es'ka'an is a Tau Fire Caste commander who led the T'au invasion force targeting the Imperial world of Pavonis. He appears in Graham McNeill's Courage and Honour as the military counterpart to the diplomatic Tau presence on that world.

Aun'rai is a Tau Ethereal who accompanied the T'au expeditionary force to Pavonis in Graham McNeill's Courage and Honour. Ethereals serve as the supreme governing caste of the T'au Empire, wielding enormous authority over warriors and diplomats alike.

Lord Winterbourne commands the Lavrentian Imperial Guard regiment deployed to Pavonis during the Tau invasion depicted in Graham McNeill's Courage and Honour. He represents the Imperial Guard's contribution to the defence of the planet alongside Uriel Ventris's Fourth Company.

Chief Librarian Varro Tigurius is the most gifted psyker in the Ultramarines Chapter, renowned across the Imperium for the accuracy of his visions. His foresight has guided the Chapter through many crises, and he serves as a senior advisor to successive Masters of Ultramar.

Ortan Cassius is the Ultramarines Master of Sanctity and its oldest active warrior, a veteran of countless campaigns spanning over a millennium. A survivor of Hive Fleet Kraken's assault on Ultramar, he became one of the Chapter's foremost authorities on Tyranid warfare.

Kydomor Forrix, called The Breaker, was a Warsmith and first captain of the Iron Warriors' 1st Grand Battalion. A member of Perturabo's inner council known as the Trident, he played a key role in the assault on Hydra Cordatus depicted in Graham McNeill's Storm of Iron.

Kroeger was an Iron Warriors Warsmith and member of the Trident whose barely-contained aggression set him apart from his more calculated brothers. He commands one of three assault forces during the siege of Hydra Cordatus in Graham McNeill's Storm of Iron.

Barban Falk, known simply as the Warsmith, led a Grand Company of the Iron Warriors in a devastating siege of the Imperial fortress world Hydra Cordatus. He is the primary villain of Graham McNeill's Storm of Iron, and his victory there sets the stage for Honsou's rise.

Iron Warriors Chaos Dreadnought and Warsmith, Berossus led a Grand Company from the daemon world Medrengard. A bitter veteran of the original Legion, he feuded with the half-breed Warsmith Honsou during the civil war on Medrengard, meeting his end when Honsou tore him from his sarcophagus.

Adeptus Mechanicus Magos attached to the Imperial garrison of Hydra Cordatus, featured in Graham McNeill's Storm of Iron. He coordinates the Mechanicus presence during the Iron Warriors siege, concealing his own agenda behind a bronze mask.

The Living Saint of the Adepta Sororitas, Celestine is an Ecclesiarchy icon believed to embody the Emperor's divine will. Slain and resurrected multiple times across M41, she rallies the Sisters of Battle and their allies in humanity's darkest moments.

Champion of Slaanesh and First Sword of the Emperor's Children, Lucius carries the unique curse that any warrior who kills him and feels even a flicker of pride in doing so is gradually consumed and transformed into a new host body for Lucius.

Warmaster of Chaos and master of the Black Legion, Abaddon is the most feared Chaos Space Marine lord of the Age of the Imperium. Former First Captain of the Luna Wolves, he led thirteen Black Crusades from the Eye of Terror, culminating in the devastating Thirteenth.

Chief Librarian of the Thousand Sons during the Great Crusade, Ahriman cast the catastrophic Rubric of Ahriman to save his Legion from the Flesh Change — instead sealing most of them within their armour as dust. Exiled by Magnus, he has since pursued forbidden sorcery across the galaxy in search of the Black Library.

Great Wolf and supreme commander of the Space Wolves Chapter, Logan Grimnar has led Fenris's warriors for over five centuries. Renowned for his fierce independence from Inquisitorial authority and his uncompromising defence of those under his protection.

Once a Chapter Master of the Dark Angels during the Great Crusade and Horus Heresy, Merir Astelan was among the Fallen scattered when Caliban was destroyed. Captured and interrogated across centuries, he offered a deeply contested account of Luther's revolt — arguing the defenders of Caliban acted out of duty, not treachery.

Grand Master of the Deathwing and one of the Dark Angels' most senior warriors, Belial commands the Chapter's elite Terminator First Company. He bears the weight of the Inner Circle's hunt for the Fallen alongside his duties leading the Deathwing in battle.

Captain of the Imperial Fists First Company and a veteran of centuries of warfare, Lysander disappeared into a Chaos Space Hulk and emerged decades later having endured captivity at the hands of the Iron Warriors. His return and the subsequent inquest became a defining test of his Chapter's loyalty.

Space Wolves Scout and notorious trickster, Lukas was rejected by every Great Company before the Thunderwolves eventually took him in. He carries a stasis bomb concealed within his chest as a final trap for any enemy who kills him — ensuring he never truly loses.

Traevelliath Sliscus, known as "the Serpent", is a Drukhari Corsair lord and commander of the Sky Serpents. Having stolen three Kabal flagships to escape Commorragh's political intrigues, he commands his raiders with equal parts charm and ruthless brutality across the stars.

Night Lords Talonmaster and former First Captain, Zso Sahaal was Konrad Curze's chosen heir, granted the Corona Nox as a symbol of succession. After his ship was trapped in a warp storm for ten millennia, he awoke in M41 to find his inheritance stolen — the central conflict of the novel Lord of the Night.

High Inquisitor of the Ordo Xenos and the only known human ever to study within the Aeldari Black Library, Czevak spent decades as a prisoner in the Webway before escaping with its secrets. His knowledge of Chaos lore made him a prime target for Ahriman's relentless pursuit, as depicted in Atlas Infernal.

Prince Yriel was once Admiral of the Iyanden fleet before his recklessness in battle led to his exile. He returned to save his craftworld from a Tyranid invasion, wielding the cursed Spear of Twilight — an act of salvation that bound his soul to the weapon's slow doom.

Chaos Space Marine captain of the Death Guard and founder of the Lords of Silence warband. One of the oldest surviving servants of Mortarion, he led the warband in the assault on Sabatine, home world of the White Consuls, after the opening of the Great Rift.

Anuradha Daaz is a chapter serf (Helot Secundus) of the Mentors Legion, serving as personal attendant to Lieutenant Commander Amadeus Kaias Incarius. She is the first-person narrator of Spear of the Emperor, chronicling the mission to the Emperor's Spears and the desperate war along the Great Rift's edge.

Lieutenant Commander of the Mentors Chapter, dispatched across the Great Rift during the Indomitus Crusade to assess the isolated Emperor's Spears. His ordeal — capture, reconstruction as a Primaris Marine, and the moral weight of complicity — drives the novel Spear of the Emperor.

Archmagos Dominus of the Adeptus Mechanicus and one of the most ancient figures in the Imperium. Tasked by Roboute Guilliman ten thousand years ago with developing new weapons and warriors, Cawl is the creator of the Primaris Space Marines and a pivotal figure in the events surrounding the 13th Black Crusade.

Tank commander of the Cadian 49th Armoured, originally from the world of Tsegoh. After losing her crew she is placed in command of a Leman Russ Demolisher nicknamed Steel Tread, thrust into a grinding offensive against Chaos forces on the war-ravaged world of Croatoas.

Inquisitor of the Ordo Malleus and the protagonist of Ian Watson's Inquisition War trilogy. A driven and increasingly unorthodox agent, Draco's pursuit of forbidden truths takes him through xenos cults, Chaos intrigue, and to the edge of sanity.

Callidus Temple assassin and member of Inquisitor Jaq Draco's retinue in the Inquisition War trilogy. A polymorphine-user trained to assume any identity, she serves as Draco's most lethal operative and develops a deeply personal bond with him over the course of the trilogy.

Navigator of House Googol and a member of Inquisitor Jaq Draco's retinue throughout the Inquisition War trilogy by Ian Watson. His warp-sight and navigational skill are essential to the warband's survival across the most hostile regions of the galaxy.

A Squat (Votann ancestor) engineer and stalwart member of Inquisitor Jaq Draco's retinue in Ian Watson's Inquisition War trilogy. His practical expertise and stubborn resilience make him an indispensable companion across the trilogy's increasingly harrowing events.

Imperial Commissar attached to the Eleventh Antari Rifles during the Bale Stars Crusade. Protagonist of Rachel Harrison's Honourbound and its associated short fiction, Raine is defined by an uncompromising sense of duty and a complicated relationship with the soldiers under her watch.

Sister Hospitaller who abandoned her Order of the Last Candle years before the events of Peter Fehervari's Requiem Infernal. She returns to the order's Candleworld on a mission of mercy, concealing a secret charge to investigate her former sisters for signs of corruption.

Decimus Androdinus Felix is an Ultramarine officer elevated to the rank of Tetrarch by Roboute Guilliman during the Indomitus Crusade. One of the earliest Primaris Space Marines, he is entrusted with the governance and eastward expansion of a realm within Ultramar.

Chettamandey Vula Brobantis is the Novator of Navigator House Brobantis and the protagonist of Mike Brooks' Rites of Passage. A skilled navigator born into House Dacastos and married into power, she is drawn into a deadly conspiracy of ritual murders and warp-related catastrophe threatening her dynasty.

Battle-Sister of the Order of Our Martyred Lady and the first-person protagonist of Rachel Harrison's Mark of Faith. Denied a martyr's death at Ophelia VII, she is instead sent beyond the Great Rift on a pilgrimage to recover the Shield of Saint Katherine.

Inquisitor of the Ordo Hereticus and a co-protagonist in Rachel Harrison's Mark of Faith. She accompanies Battle-Sister Evangeline on the pilgrimage beyond the Great Rift, driven in part by a personal vendetta against a Thousand Sons sorcerer who crippled her former interrogator.

A Battle Sister of the Order of Our Martyred Lady, Ephrael Stern was reborn three times by the souls of her slaughtered sisters, earning the title Daemonifuge. Her uncanny ability to destroy daemons through touch brands her a heretic in the eyes of the Inquisition even as she remains a weapon against Chaos.

A renegade Harlequin known as Kyganil of the Bloody Tears, he spent centuries as a nameless outcast after deserting his troupe. Prophecy eventually sent him to serve as guardian and companion to Ephrael Stern, earning the title Knight of Shadows among his kin.

A Bad Moons Ork Warboss renowned for bringing down enemy war-engines, Ufthak Blackhawk is the brutal, cunning protagonist of the novel Brutal Kunnin'. His campaign against a Adeptus Mechanicus forge world establishes him as a rising power among the greenskins and sparks Waaagh! Ufthak.

Overlord of the Nihilakh Dynasty and self-styled galactic archivist, Trazyn the Infinite obsessively collects unique specimens — living beings included — for his vast underground museums on Solemnace. His indirect meddling in major events of the 41st Millennium, often for purely acquisitive reasons, makes him one of the most unpredictable Necron lords.

Chief Astronomer of the Sautekh Dynasty, Orikan the Diviner possesses an extraordinary gift for precognition, allowing him to calculate and manipulate the outcomes of battles long before they begin. His bitter rivalry with Trazyn the Infinite is a recurring thread through the Necron novels of C.S. Goto and Mike Brooks.

Chief Librarian of the Carcharodons Astra and bearer of the title Pale Nomad, Te Kahurangi is one of the oldest Space Marines alive, only three generations removed from the Chapter's founding exiles. His formidable psychic power and ancient lineage make him a central figure in the Red Tithe and Silent Hunters novels.

Chaplain of the Carcharodons Astra and twin brother to Te Kahurangi, Tangata Manu spent over a millennium searching for a sacred Chapter relic — the Void Glass — whose loss he blamed on himself. His obsessive quest drives the novel Silent Hunters and ultimately takes him into the depths of Commorragh.

An Ultramarines Captain commanding the strike cruiser Ithraca's Vengeance within Indomitus Crusade Fleet Quintus, Aeschelus serves as the primary POV character of Gav Thorpe's novel Indomitus. Relatively inexperienced, he leads his forces against Necron overlord Simut during a crisis that traps the fleet in a warp-becalmed system.

An ace fighter pilot of the Aeronautica Imperialis, Lucille von Shard is the lead protagonist of Denny Flowers' Outgunned and its sequel Above and Beyond. Celebrated for her role in defending the agri world Bacchus from an Ork invasion, she later grapples with fame, burnout, and the gap between Imperial propaganda and truth.

An Imperial propagandist dispatched to produce a pict-record glorifying ace pilot Lucille von Shard, Kile Simlex appears in Denny Flowers' Outgunned and Above and Beyond. His insistence on depicting the unglamorous reality of war lands him in disgrace, even as his films inspire mass recruitment across the Imperium.

A veteran Aeronautica Imperialis ace, Bree Jagdea first appears in Dan Abnett's Double Eagle as a squadron commander during the Sabbat Worlds Crusade. Decades later in the sequel Interceptor City, she is a burned-out pilot dragged back into combat to fly death-defying interception runs through the ruins of Vesperus.

Daughter of legendary Cadian Lord Castellan Ursarkar Creed, Ursula carries the weight of her father's legacy after the Fall of Cadia. She is the POV protagonist of Jude Reid's novel Creed: Ashes of Cadia, fighting to preserve what remains of Cadian identity and purpose among the world's displaced survivors.

Lord Castellan of Cadia and one of the Imperium's most celebrated military minds, Ursarkar E. Creed commanded the planet's last defence against Abaddon's 13th Black Crusade. His fate during the Fall of Cadia — and the legendary tactical genius he displayed to the end — remains a defining moment in recent 40K lore.

An Inquisitor of the Ordo Hereticus, Astor Sabbathiel is notable for returning from an apparent death a century after her disappearance. She is the protagonist of George Mann's novel Awakenings and the short fiction collected in Pilgrims of Fire, her resurrection and unorthodox methods placing her at odds with her own Order.

A Space Wolves warrior and former member of the rival Grimskull tribe, Strybjorn begins as an enemy of Ragnar Blackmane before becoming his pack-brother. He features across William King's Ragnar saga, his arc tracing a shift from bitter tribal rivalry to brotherhood forged through shared battle.

Space Wolf Blood Claw who serves alongside Ragnar Blackmane as a pack-brother through the early books of William King's saga. Brash and aggressive even by Fenrisian standards, he is a recurring presence in Ragnar's formative campaigns.

Wolf Priest of the Space Wolves who serves as mentor and spiritual guide to Ragnar Blackmane's pack in William King's saga. As a Wolf Priest he is responsible for testing aspirants and upholding the traditions of Fenris.

Blood Claw pack-brother of Ragnar Blackmane who succumbs to the Curse of the Wulfen early in William King's Space Wolf novel. His transformation and death at Ragnar's hands is one of the defining formative moments of the saga.

Wolf Lord of the Space Wolves and commanding officer of Ragnar Blackmane's Great Company. A veteran warrior of enormous renown, he acts as Ragnar's patron and superior across multiple novels in William King's saga.

Imperial Inquisitor who employs Ragnar Blackmane's pack in Ragnar's Claw, tasking them with recovering the Talisman of Lykos. He reappears across the broader saga as a recurring quest-giver and representative of the Inquisition's interests.

Thousand Sons Chaos Sorcerer and the primary arch-antagonist of William King's Space Wolf saga. Acting on behalf of Magnus the Red's designs, Madox pursues schemes spanning Grey Hunter, Wolfblade, and Wolf's Honour that bring him into repeated conflict with Ragnar Blackmane.

Navigator of House Belisarius who becomes bonded to Ragnar Blackmane and accompanies him through his time on Terra in Wolfblade. Her Navis Nobilite lineage and political connections prove as vital as any weapon in the intrigue-heavy storylines of the Second Omnibus arc.

Veteran Space Wolf and member of the Wolfblade who serves alongside Ragnar Blackmane during his posting on Terra. Experienced and sardonic, Torin acts as a guide to the political machinations of the Terran court and the duties of the Wolfblade.

Wolf Lord of the Space Wolves and the central POV character of Andy Lee's Krakenblood (2025). He leads a Great Company defined by its ferocity, confronting threats that test the limits of Space Wolf brotherhood and honour.

Ancient and revered Wolf Priest of the Space Wolves, one of the oldest warriors in the Chapter. A canonical lore figure dating to third edition, Ulrik appears in Krakenblood as a quest-giver, lending the novel a connection to the Chapter's deepest traditions.

Underhive bounty hunter operating in Hive Primus on Necromunda, and the illegitimate son of Lord Helmawr. Protagonist of the classic Kal Jerico trilogy and the later Sinner's Bounty, he is defined by his quick wit, sharpshooting, and reluctant heroism in Necromunda's deadly depths.

Kal Jerico's loyal cyber-mastiff and constant companion throughout the classic Kal Jerico trilogy. A heavily augmented combat hound, Wotan provides both protection and a dash of dark comedy in the treacherous Underhive.

A half-ratskin Underhive denizen who serves as Kal Jerico's scavenging sidekick in the classic trilogy. Scabbs is equal parts unlucky and resourceful, providing comic relief and local knowledge as the two navigate Hive Primus's most dangerous levels.

A psyker who accompanies Kal Jerico as a companion during the classic Kal Jerico trilogy. Her abilities offer the group an edge in the lawless Underhive, though psykers are rarely trusted or safe in Necromunda's dangerous society.

Spire-Lord of Hive Primus and ruler of Necromunda's dominant noble house in the classic-continuity novels. His assassination during the events of Lasgun Wedding marks the climax of the original Kal Jerico trilogy, distinguishing him from the lord depicted in later soft-rebooted continuity.

Planetary Governor and Spire-Lord of Hive Primus on Necromunda, ruler of House Helmawr. His authority over the planet is near-absolute, enforced through the Palanite Enforcers and layers of noble obligation.

High Hierophant of the Redemption Cult active in Hive Primus's underhive, and Kal Jerico's most persistent nemesis. After surviving a plunge into a toxic pool, he declared himself reborn by the Emperor's will and declared Jerico the greatest of all heretics.

D'onne Ulanti — 'Mad Donna' — is a Spire-born exile of House Ulanti who descended to the underhive and became a legend among House Escher gangs. Notoriously violent and unpredictable, she is the quarry at the centre of the chase in Soulless Fury.

A Ratskin warrior whose tribe was massacred in the Blood River Massacre, Iktomi survives as the lethally capable partner of self-styled underhive legend Caleb Cursebound. Cold and bound by a survivor's debt, she is the true killing force behind Caleb's inflated reputation.

Self-proclaimed ninth most dangerous man in the underhive, Caleb is a con artist and bounty hunter whose reputation vastly outstrips his actual violence. His survival relies on misdirection, fast talk, and the deadly competence of his Ratskin partner Iktomi.

Daughter of Red Tori, leader of the Wild Hydras Escher gang, Brielle is cast into the deepest underhive when Goliath overlord Fettnir destroys her family. Terminal Overkill follows her years-long coming-of-age journey from fugitive to avenger.

An elite Palanite Scrutinator-Primus and psychic null feared across Necromunda for dismantling criminal organisations. Armed with Lord Helmawr's personal seal in Soulless Fury, she pursues Mad Donna through the underhive accompanied by her cyber-mastiff KB-88.

An Escher ganger of the Wild Cats who is forced to take command after a Guilder ambush goes wrong and outlaws her gang. The protagonist of Wanted: Dead, she fights to protect her surviving sisters, driven by loyalty and love for her partner Quinne.

Officio Prefectus officer commanding the 13th Penal Legion — the Last Chancers — across Gav Thorpe's five-novel series. Schaeffer recruits the condemned and expendable for suicide missions, with freedom promised to any who survive.

The Last Chancers' primary POV narrator across the Thorpe series, a convicted soldier with latent psyker abilities pressed into Schaeffer's penal legion. By Armageddon Saint he has become a figure of saint-cult legend known as the Burned Man.

A marksman and one of a pair of twins serving in Schaeffer's Last Chancers, Lorii features prominently in Annihilation Squad as one of the penal legion's most capable operatives. Her bond with her sibling runs through the squad's fragile loyalty.

The treacherous Imperial Commander of Armageddon who abandoned his post during the Second War for Armageddon and retreated into the ork-infested Hive Acheron. A renegade governor and arch-antagonist, he is the target of the Last Chancers in Annihilation Squad.

Flag-captain of the Imperial Navy cruiser Lord Solar Macharius during the Gothic War, Sempter is the dual POV anchor of Gordon Rennie's Gothic War duology. He navigates fleet politics and void combat across Execution Hour and Shadow Point.

Chapter Master of the Soul Drinkers, Sarpedon leads his renegade Chapter through Ben Counter's six-novel series following their break from the Adeptus Mechanicus. His mutation — arachnid lower limbs — and quest to preserve the Chapter's genetic purity define the arc.

Ancient philosopher-warrior of the Soul Drinkers whose foundational writings shaped the Chapter's doctrine for millennia. The Daenyathos novella and Phalanx reveal that he manipulated the Chapter's entire history from within, making him its most dangerous hidden antagonist.

Chaplain and Master of Sanctity of the Soul Drinkers, secretly the most devoted instrument of the philosopher-warrior Daenyathos. Iktinos manipulated the Chapter's path toward damnation from within, serving as the hidden architect of its excommunication. He meets his end aboard the Imperial Fists fortress-monastery Phalanx.

A Soul Drinkers assault sergeant whose battlefield frenzy escalated into daemonic mutation after the Chapter's fall from Imperial grace. Tellos abandoned all restraint, becoming consumed by bloodlust and physical corruption. His arc across the early Soul Drinkers novels embodies the cost of the Chapter's renegade path.

A rogue psyker and self-styled plague-lord who carved out a spreading empire of disease and undead servitude across Imperial space. Born from Adeptus Mechanicus mutation experiments, Teturact is the primary antagonist of The Bleeding Chalice and the first major threat faced by Sarpedon's excommunicated Chapter.

An Inquisitor assigned to hunt down the renegade Soul Drinkers after their excommunication. In The Bleeding Chalice, his pursuit of Sarpedon leads him into unlikely cooperation against Teturact's plague empire. His Ordo affiliation is not explicitly confirmed in the text.

A post-Cicatrix Maledictum arch-heretic and Chaos cult leader who serves as the primary antagonist in Traitor by Deed, the Primaris-era continuation of the Soul Drinkers storyline. Yeceqath represents the new scale of Chaos threat that the reborn Soul Drinkers must face in the era of the Great Rift.

Arbiter Senioris of the Adeptus Arbites, posted to the fleet-fortress system of Hydraphur to enforce Imperial law among its powerful noble houses and religious factions. The protagonist of Matthew Farrer's Crossfire trilogy, she stands out as an early female POV lead navigating political corruption and assassination plots through rigorous Imperial jurisprudence.

Head of a noble house within the Hydraphur system and a corrupt antagonist in Matthew Farrer's Crossfire. Medell manipulates the system's political machinery for personal gain, drawing the attention of Arbiter Senioris Shira Calpurnia.

A Rogue Trader whose death sets the events of Matthew Farrer's Legacy in motion. Phrax's valuable charter becomes the prize in a contest among rival heirs, and Arbiter Senioris Calpurnia is charged with ensuring his will is honoured according to Imperial law.

Justicar of the Grey Knights and the protagonist of Ben Counter's Grey Knights trilogy. Alaric is noted for unconventional tactical thinking within his daemon-hunting brotherhood, and faces trials ranging from a Tzeentchian daemon prince's resurrection to captivity on a Chaos daemon world dedicated to Khorne.

Chapter Master of the Blood Ravens and one of the most celebrated Space Marine commanders in the Ultima Segmentum. Angelos rose from Captain of the 3rd Company, bearing a deep personal guilt over his order to enact Exterminatus on his own homeworld of Cyrene. He is the central figure of the Dawn of War game and novel series.

Farseer of Craftworld Biel-Tan and a recurring figure across the Dawn of War series. Macha leads Biel-Tan's Swordwind forces on Tartarus to prevent the Maledictum — a daemon-prison artefact — from being disturbed by other factions. She returns in Dawn of War III during the conflict over Acheron.

A Blood Angels Space Marine and the protagonist of James Swallow's Blood Angels novel series. Rafen is tested most severely when his brother Arkio claims to be the reincarnation of their Primarch, forcing him to choose between faith and loyalty. He later confronts the machinations of the Chaos renegade Fabius Bile.

Marshal of the Solemnus Crusade of the Black Templars and the commanding figure of Jonathan Green's Armageddon duology. Brant leads his crusade in a years-long campaign of vengeance after his Chapter's homeworld is devastated by Orks, ultimately pursuing retribution to the hive world of Armageddon.

Inquisitor Lord of the Ordo Malleus and the superior officer directing Justicar Alaric's squad in Ben Counter's Grey Knights trilogy. A veteran of centuries of daemonic investigation, Nyxos is physically frail but sharp-minded, sustained by a servo-assisted exoskeleton. He dispatches the Grey Knights to prevent the resurrection of the daemon prince Ghargatuloth.

A Blood Angels Space Marine whose apparent miracles and physical transformation lead many within the Chapter to believe he is the reincarnation of the Primarch Sanguinius. Arkio is the catalyst for a devastating schism in James Swallow's Deus Encarmine and Deus Sanguinius, with his brother Rafen the sole dissenting voice.

Trooper Arvin Larn is a newly conscripted Guardsman in the 902nd Vardan Rifles, protagonist of Mitchel Scanlon's novel Fifteen Hours. Misrouted to the wrong warzone, he is thrown into the siege of Broucheroc, where the average life expectancy of a replacement trooper is just fifteen hours.

First Captain Michaelus Raphael commanded the Blood Angels 1st Company in the iconic cleansing of the Space Hulk Sin of Damnation circa 596.M41. Leading eighty Terminator-armoured veterans, he oversaw the destruction of more than forty thousand Genestealers aboard the hulk.

Captain Sebastev of the Vostroyan 68th Regiment is the protagonist of Steve Parker's novel Rebel Winter. When a withdrawal on the ice world of Danik's World goes catastrophically wrong, Sebastev must lead his cut-off company through enemy lines while escorting a valuable prisoner to Imperial command.

Duke Venalitor is a Chaos warlord and servant of Khorne, lord of the daemon world Drakaasi, who features as the primary antagonist in Ben Counter's novel Hammer of Daemons. He commanded an invasion of the Imperial world Sarthis Majoris during the Thirteenth Black Crusade.

Ghargatuloth, the Prince of a Thousand Faces, is a mighty Daemon Prince of Tzeentch whose banishment forms the central conflict of Ben Counter's Grey Knights novel. Known by dozens of epithets, he manifests as a vast pillar of shifting flesh and was ultimately undone when his true name was spoken aloud by Justicar Alaric.

Isador Akios was the Librarian of the Blood Ravens 3rd Company and a close companion of Captain Gabriel Angelos, depicted in the Dawn of War game and its novelisation. On the planet Tartarus he was seduced by the Chaos sorcerer Sindri Myr into pursuing forbidden power, ultimately betraying his Chapter and dying at the hands of Angelos himself.

Major Zane Mortensen commands the Redemption Corps, an elite air-mobile Stormtrooper regiment operating across the Kaligari Cradle warzones, in Rob Sanders' novel of the same name. Grievously burned when his homeworld was destroyed, Mortensen is driven by an iron will and an almost supernatural resistance to pain.

Brother-Sergeant Noxx is a Flesh Tearers Space Marine who appears in James Swallow's Blood Angels novel Red Fury. He fights alongside Blood Angel Rafen during the hunt for Fabius Bile's stolen vials of Sanguinius's blood, embodying the barely leashed fury and savage edge of his successor Chapter.

Captain Parmenion Thade leads the Cadian 88th Armoured Regiment in Aaron Dembski-Bowden's novel Cadian Blood, fighting a desperate reclamation campaign on the plague-infected world of Kathur. His actions during the campaign earn him promotion to Warden Colonel, a title he is reluctant to accept.

Inquisitor Lord Ramius Stele was a renegade of the Ordo Hereticus who served as the hidden antagonist of James Swallow's Blood Angels duology. Having made a pact with the daemon Malfallax and the Word Bearers, he manipulated the Blood Angel Arkio into posing as Sanguinius reborn to deliver the Chapter to Chaos.

Rhamah was a Librarian of the Blood Ravens and wielder of the legendary force blade Vairocanum. After suffering amnesia during a mission to Arcadia, he fell under the influence of Thousand Sons Sorcerer Ahriman and was ultimately slain by a Harlequin Solitaire before he could be recovered by his Chapter.

Sergeant 'Hardhead' Greiss is a veteran Catachan Jungle Fighter featured in Steve Lyons' novel Death World. His squad is tasked with a covert infiltration of an Ork camp to eliminate the enemy warboss, relying on the brutal fieldcraft that makes Catachan soldiers legendary throughout the Guard.

Sergeant Lorenzo is a Blood Angels 1st Company veteran and one of the iconic figures of the Space Hulk campaign set aboard the Sin of Damnation. A survivor of a disastrous boarding action six centuries prior, he led the Terminator strike force that ultimately purged the hulk of its Genestealer infestation.

Sergeant Oskar Wulfe commands the Leman Russ tank Last Rites in the Cadian 81st Armoured Regiment, protagonist of Steve Parker's novel Gunheads. His company is despatched to the hostile world of Golgottha on a mission to recover the legendary tank Fortress of Arrogance, once belonging to Commissar Yarrick.

Astorath the Grim is the High Chaplain of the Blood Angels, known as the Redeemer of the Lost. He travels ceaselessly between the Blood Angels and their successor Chapters to grant the Emperor's Peace to those brothers consumed by the Black Rage, an honour and a burden no other Space Marine bears.

Colonel of the 2nd Catachan Jungle Fighters, Straken is renowned as one of the most physically formidable officers in the Astra Militarum. Half his body replaced with bionics after a monstrous mauling, he remains a ferocious close-combat fighter and beloved leader of his regiment.

An Imperial Commissar assigned to the newly reformed 77th Vostroyan Firstborn, Flint is the protagonist of Andy Hoare's novel Commissar. Deployed to the prison world of Furia Penitens, he must restore the regiment's shattered reputation while suppressing a violent uprising.

A by-the-book Imperial Commissar thrust into command of a Savlar Chem-Dogs kill team in Callum Davis's novel Chem Dog. Far from the elite troops he expected, Hasp must overcome his contempt for his penal-legion charges if any of them are to survive their mission behind enemy lines.

A Night Lords Chaos Space Marine lord known as the Skin-Taker, Dalchian Rassaq commands the Blades of Atrocity warband in Mike Vincent's The Remnant Blade. Betrayed and stripped of his standing, he schemes to outmanoeuvre rivals and reclaim dominance over his fractious warriors.

Tempestor commanding the First Eradicant Scions in R.S. Wilt's Final Deployment, Traxel leads a squad of battle-scarred misfit operatives into a civil war on the planet Rilis. He must hold his damaged unit together against both traitor armies and the Iron Warriors they have allied with.

An Cadian armour officer whose full name is Marken Cortein Lo Bannick, he serves as the POV protagonist of Guy Haley's tank duology — debuting as a young Baneblade commander in Baneblade and continuing through the brutal siege warfare of Shadowsword. His arc charts guilt, duty, and the grinding cost of armoured warfare.

Chapter Master of the Blood Angels and one of the longest-lived Space Marine commanders in the Imperium, Dante has led his Chapter for over a thousand years. He appears in the Blood Angels novels including Deus Encarmine and Astorath, embodying the tragic nobility of his lineage.

A Night Lords Chaos Space Marine and prophet of the First Claw, Talos is the central figure of Aaron Dembski-Bowden's Night Lords trilogy. Haunted by visions and a darkly principled hatred of the Imperium, he struggles to hold his warband together across a lifetime of raids and bitter rearguard warfare.

A member of First Claw in Aaron Dembski-Bowden's Night Lords trilogy, Cyrion is a sardonic and self-aware Chaos Space Marine whose wry humour masks a genuine curse — a psychic hunger that compels him to feed on the emotions of the dying.

One of the First Claw warriors in the Night Lords trilogy by Aaron Dembski-Bowden, Uzas is a berserker progressively consumed by devotion to Khorne. His ever-worsening bloodlust makes him both weapon and liability to his brothers throughout the series.

A First Claw Night Lord in Aaron Dembski-Bowden's trilogy, Xarl is the squad's most straightforwardly martial member — a proud, skilled fighter who takes satisfaction in the craft of killing. His blunt pragmatism contrasts with Talos's brooding idealism.

The heavy weapons specialist of First Claw in Aaron Dembski-Bowden's Night Lords series, Mercutian favours bolter and devastator-class firepower. He is among the more measured voices in the warband, though no less capable of brutality when called upon.

A human artificer and bondsman serving the Night Lords warband of Talos, Septimus maintains the warband's armour and equipment throughout Aaron Dembski-Bowden's trilogy. His perspective as a mortal among monsters gives the series one of its few grounded, human points of view.

A Navigator pressed into service aboard the Night Lords vessel in Aaron Dembski-Bowden's trilogy, Octavia guides the warband's ship through the warp. Her relationship with Septimus forms a rare thread of humanity within a crew defined by cruelty.

Once the Night Lords captain known as Vandred, the Exalted is a Chaos Space Marine whose body has been seized by a possessing daemon, warping him into a hulking, serpentine horror. He commands Talos's warband in Soul Hunter and remains a brooding, volatile authority throughout the early Night Lords novels.

Tsu'gan is a Salamanders Space Marine Sergeant featured throughout Nick Kyme's Tome of Fire trilogy. Fierce and proud, he serves as a foil to Dak'ir, embodying a more aggressive philosophy within the Chapter. His rivalry and personal struggles drive much of the trilogy's character drama.

Dak'ir is a Salamanders Space Marine and one of the central protagonists of Nick Kyme's Tome of Fire trilogy. Possessed of latent psychic ability and an unusually empathetic nature for an Astartes, he grapples with his destiny and the mysteries of Nocturne throughout the series.

Elysius is a Chaplain of the Salamanders Chapter, appearing in Nick Kyme's Tome of Fire series. As a keeper of the Chapter's spiritual traditions and warrior culture, he guides and tests his battle-brothers through the trials depicted in the novels.

Nihilan is a renegade Salamander turned Chaos Space Marine and the primary antagonist of Nick Kyme's Tome of Fire trilogy. As leader of the Dragon Warriors warband, he harbours a deep hatred for his former Chapter and pursues vengeance with ruthless ambition.

Marduk is a Dark Apostle of the Word Bearers Traitor Legion and the protagonist of Anthony Reynolds' Dark Apostle trilogy. A fanatical orator and champion of Chaos Undivided, he leads his Host on a brutal crusade to enact the will of the Dark Gods.

Kol Badar is the Coryphaus — second-in-command — of Marduk's Word Bearers Host in Anthony Reynolds' Dark Apostle trilogy. A veteran warrior of immense brutality, he enforces the Dark Apostle's will on the battlefield and harbours his own fierce ambitions.

Burias is the Icon Bearer of Marduk's Word Bearers Host in Anthony Reynolds' Dark Apostle trilogy. Bonded with a powerful daemon that grants him terrifying strength in battle, he is among the most devoted and dangerous warriors in Marduk's warband.

Jarulek is a Dark Apostle of the Word Bearers and Marduk's superior at the start of Anthony Reynolds' Dark Apostle series. His brutal methods and singular devotion to the Chaos Gods set the trilogy in motion, establishing the political tensions within the Word Bearers Host.

Lucian Gerrit is a Rogue Trader and the central protagonist of Andy Hoare's Rogue Star trilogy. As patriarch of the Gerrit dynasty, he navigates shifting alliances on the Eastern Fringe, balancing Imperial duty, family loyalty, and the treacherous politics of frontier space.

Brielle Gerrit is the daughter of Rogue Trader Lucian Gerrit and a key character in Andy Hoare's Rogue Star trilogy. Headstrong and independent, she pursues her own agenda on the Eastern Fringe, leading her into dangerous entanglements that complicate her father's mission.

Inquisitor Grand is an antagonist in Andy Hoare's Rogue Star trilogy, acting as an Imperial Inquisitor who clashes with the Gerrit Rogue Trader dynasty. His interference and investigation of the Gerrits' activities creates significant obstacles throughout the series.

Sarik is a White Scars Space Marine Sergeant featured in Andy Hoare's Rogue Star series. Attached to operations on the Eastern Fringe, he provides the Adeptus Astartes perspective alongside the Rogue Trader narrative, bringing the Chapter's swift and aggressive combat style to the story.

Sister Miriya is a Battle Sister of the Adepta Sororitas and the protagonist of James Swallow's Faith and Fire and Hammer and Anvil novels. A warrior of fierce conviction, she is defined by her unwavering faith and the personal trials that test her devotion to the Emperor.

Sister Verity is a Sister Hospitaller and companion to Miriya in James Swallow's Adepta Sororitas novels. As a healer rather than a warrior, she offers a contrasting perspective on faith, mercy, and the cost of war within the Sisters of Battle narrative.

Grimaldus is the Reclusiarch of the Black Templars and the hero of Aaron Dembski-Bowden's Helsreach. He led the Chapter's defence of Hive Helsreach during the Third War for Armageddon, becoming a legendary figure known as the Hero of Helsreach for his defiance against overwhelming ork forces.

High Marshal of the Black Templars, the supreme commander of the crusading Space Marine Chapter. He leads with uncompromising zeal, directing multiple crusades simultaneously across the galaxy in the Emperor's name.

Chaos Space Marine warlord and self-proclaimed Tyrant of Badab, leader of the Red Corsairs warband. Once a loyalist Chapter Master, his rebellion ended in defeat and transformation into one of the most powerful renegade lords in the Maelstrom.

Master of the Hunt of the White Scars, tasked with tracking and slaying those who flee Chapter justice. He led the celebrated hunt that brought down the daemon prince Voldorius and fought during the Damocles Crusade against the T'au.

Space Marine of the Blood Angels Legion known as the Flesh Tearer for his ferocity in close combat, and the founder of the Flesh Tearers successor Chapter. His violent reputation made him a feared warrior even among his own brothers.

Ork Warboss and Weirdboy featured in the Sanctus Reach campaign setting. He led Ork forces in the brutal war for the Sanctus Reach system, combining brute battlefield leadership with unpredictable psychic power.

Chapter Master of the Crimson Fists, one of the most tested leaders in the Imperium. He guided his Chapter's survival following the near-destruction of their fortress monastery during the Ork invasion of Rynn's World.

Great Wolf of the Space Wolves, supreme commander of the Chapter, who led his forces during the Battle of the Fang against Magnus the Red and the Thousand Sons. His obsessive hunt for Magnus defined much of his tenure as Great Wolf.

Greater Daemon of Tzeentch known as the Oracle of Tzeentch, possessing knowledge of all possible futures except his own. He appears in the Architect of Fate anthology as a manipulator of mortal and immortal pawns across multiple interlocking plots.

Captain of the Blood Angels 1st Company, the Flesh Tearers' veteran elite. He commanded the Blood Angels' defence during the Shield of Baal campaign against the devastating Tyranid Leviathan splinter fleet.

Renegade pyromancer and primary antagonist of James Swallow's Faith and Fire. A powerful psyker and heretic hunted by the Sisters of Battle, his volatile nature and crimes against the Imperium drive the central conflict of the novel.

Red Corsairs Apothecary known as the Flayer, a central figure in Aaron Dembski-Bowden's Night Lords trilogy. His brutal surgical skills and cold pragmatism make him a morally complex presence among the Night Lords warband.

Supreme Commander of the T'au Fire Caste and the foremost military leader of the T'au Empire, protégé of Commander Farsight. She directed major T'au campaigns including operations during the Damocles Crusade and the Third Sphere of Expansion.

Sister Superior of the Order of the Bloody Rose and the protagonist of Danie Ware's novella series. Her faith and martial resolve are tested across a series of increasingly desperate missions in service to the Emperor.

Inquisitor and central protagonist of Henry Zou's Bastion Wars series, beginning with Emperor's Mercy. He investigates the mysterious threat of the Archenemy across the war-torn Eastern Fringe, navigating political intrigue and relentless combat.

Ork Warboss and central antagonist of the Sanctus Reach campaign, commanding a vast Waaagh! through the Sanctus Reach system. His savage drive and raw brutality made him one of the most dangerous Ork leaders of his era.

An Aeldari of Craftworld Alaitoc, Korlandril abandons his life as an artist and sculptor to walk the Path of the Warrior as a Striking Scorpion. His growing mastery of the Aspect leads him toward the irreversible threshold of the Exarch. Central POV character in Gav Thorpe's Path of the Warrior.

An Aeldari of Craftworld Alaitoc and a former friend of Korlandril, Thirianna leaves the Path of the Poet to walk the perilous Path of the Seer. She develops her farseer abilities while navigating the labyrinthine visions of the skein. POV character in Gav Thorpe's Path of the Seer.

An Aeldari of Craftworld Alaitoc who abandons the rigid structure of the Path to become an Outcast, eventually rising to lead a corsair warband. His wandering life brings him into conflict far beyond craftworld space. POV character in Gav Thorpe's Path of the Outcast.

A Mandrake of Commorragh serving as a deadly assassin within the schemes of Andy Chambers' Path of the Dark Eldar trilogy. Mandrakes are shadowy killers who can melt between darkness and reality; Morr is among the most lethal figures accompanying Nyos Yllithian's ambitions.

An Archon of Commorragh and the central POV character of Andy Chambers' Path of the Dark Eldar trilogy. Yllithian schemes through the treacherous politics of the Dark City, pursuing power by attempting to resurrect a long-dead El'Uriaq in a desperate bid to upset the balance of Commorrite rule.

A Haemonculus of Commorragh whose mastery of flesh-craft makes him a sought-after and dangerous figure in Andy Chambers' Path of the Dark Eldar series. His surgical and alchemical skills place him at the heart of Yllithian's resurrection scheme, though his own agenda is rarely simple.

Lord Solar Macharius was one of the Imperium's greatest Imperial Guard commanders, leading the Macharian Crusade in the 41st Millennium and conquering nearly a thousand worlds in seven years. His campaigns are compared in scope only to those of the Primarchs. Subject of William King's Macharian Crusade trilogy.

A Commissar assigned to the forces of Lord Solar Macharius, Leo Lemuel serves as the chronicler and narrator of the Macharian Crusade in William King's trilogy. His personal accounts of the crusade's triumphs and eventual fracture form the backbone of the narrative.

A soldier of the Praetorian Guard serving under Lord Solar Macharius during the Macharian Crusade. Anton is a recurring ensemble member in William King's trilogy, providing a ground-level perspective on the crusade's vast military campaigns alongside his comrade Ivan.

A Praetorian Guard soldier and close companion of Anton in William King's Macharian Crusade trilogy. Ivan is part of the enlisted ensemble surrounding Leo Lemuel, experiencing the crusade's glory and grim costs from the perspective of an ordinary fighting man.

An Inquisitor attached to the forces of Lord Solar Macharius during the Macharian Crusade in William King's trilogy. Drake represents the Inquisition's ever-present watchfulness over even the Imperium's greatest commanders, serving as a morally complex figure within Macharius's inner circle.

A Praetorian Guard soldier featuring in the later stages of William King's Macharian Crusade series, particularly in Fall of Macharius. Richter is part of the ground-level ensemble that witnesses the crusade's unravelling in the wake of Macharius's death.

Archmagos Lexell Kotov of the Adeptus Mechanicus leads the expeditionary fleet aboard the Ark Mechanicus Speranza in Graham McNeill's Forges of Mars trilogy. His quest to reach the legendary forge world Exnihlio beyond the galactic fringe drives the trilogy's central narrative.

Magos Tarkis Blaylock serves as the second-in-command and a key senior figure aboard the Speranza during Archmagos Kotov's expedition in Graham McNeill's Forges of Mars trilogy. His rigid adherence to Mechanicus doctrine often puts him in tension with Kotov's more unorthodox decisions.

A Rogue Trader and captain of the vessel Renard, Roboute Surcouf joins Archmagos Kotov's expedition into uncharted space in Graham McNeill's Forges of Mars trilogy. His pragmatic and mercantile outlook contrasts with the Mechanicus mindset, providing a distinctly human perspective aboard the Speranza.

Mechanicus Adept and daughter of Vitali Tychon, she serves aboard the Ark Mechanicus Speranza during the Forges of Mars expedition. A skilled researcher, her relationship with Magos Vitali and her growing bond with Roboute Surcouf are central threads of McNeill's trilogy.

An abominable intelligence — a forbidden proto-AI — secretly embedded within the Ark Mechanicus Speranza. Originally constructed by Archmagos Telok, Galatea manipulates events from within the ship's systems and serves as a primary antagonist throughout McNeill's Forges of Mars trilogy.

Cadian Colonel commanding the 71st Cadian Regiment, assigned to escort and protect the Adeptus Mechanicus expedition aboard the Speranza in McNeill's Forges of Mars series. A steady and pragmatic officer, he represents the Imperial Guard contingent throughout the voyage.

A Black Templars Reclusiarch leading the Crusade forces embarked aboard the Ark Mechanicus Speranza in McNeill's Priests of Mars. As the ship's spiritual warrior-commander, he upholds the crusading traditions of his Chapter during the perilous deep-space expedition.

Once a celebrated Archmagos of the Adeptus Mechanicus, Telok vanished beyond the Halo Scar in search of the Breath of the Gods. Rediscovered in McNeill's Gods of Mars, he has become a Dark Mechanicum renegade of immense power and the trilogy's chief antagonist.

A Thousand Sons sorcerer and member of Ahriman's exile cabal, Ctesias specialises in binding and trafficking with daemons. He appears throughout John French's Ahriman trilogy as an untrustworthy but valuable ally, his expertise in daemonology making him indispensable despite his self-serving nature.

A renegade Space Marine of the Howling Griffons Chapter who becomes bound to Ahriman's wandering cabal in French's Ahriman: Exile and Sorcerer. Drawn into the world of sorcery against his instincts, he struggles to retain his identity while serving alongside the Thousand Sons outcasts.

A Mechanicum tech-priestess who serves as the Mistress of Astropaths and ship-mistress within Ahriman's exile cabal in French's Ahriman series. Though Mechanicum-born, she operates fully within the cabal's structure, her mastery of voidcraft making her essential to the group's survival.

An Inquisitor of the Ordo Hereticus who relentlessly pursues Ahriman across John French's Exile and Sorcerer novels. Driven by a fierce conviction to bring the Thousand Sons sorcerer to judgement, she represents the Imperium's determined but often futile efforts to contain his power.

Hero of Hades Hive and one of the Imperium's most celebrated Commissars, Yarrick earned undying fame during the Second War for Armageddon against Ghazghkull's Ork Waaagh!. His personal vendetta with Ghazghkull spans decades and is the heart of David Annandale's Yarrick trilogy.

The self-proclaimed Prophet of the Waaagh! and the mightiest Ork Warboss in the galaxy, Ghazghkull Thraka launched two devastating invasions of Armageddon. His centuries-long rivalry with Commissar Yarrick is a defining conflict of the 41st Millennium.

A Lord Commissar who serves as an early mentor to Sebastian Yarrick in Annandale's Imperial Creed. His influence helps shape Yarrick's understanding of duty, faith, and the brutal pragmatism required of an Imperial Commissar operating on a corruption-ridden world.

A Wolf Guard of the Vlka Fenryka and Jarl of the Járnhamar pack, Gunnlaugur is the blunt, ferocious heart of Chris Wraight's Blood of Asaheim trilogy. His leadership is tested as the pack is reunited and thrust into the grinding war against the Plague Marines on Ras Shakeh.

A Space Wolf of the Járnhamar pack who spent years detached on a solo mission for the Deathwatch before returning to his brothers in Wraight's Blood of Asaheim. His reintegration — changed by his long absence — is a core tension throughout the trilogy.

Master of Sanctity and the most feared Interrogator-Chaplain of the Dark Angels, Asmodai is relentless in his pursuit of the Fallen. His uncompromising zeal and brutal methods feature prominently in Gav Thorpe's Legacy of Caliban trilogy, where the Chapter's darkest secrets are at stake.

Ravenwing Black Knight of the Dark Angels, Annael is a point-of-view character in Gav Thorpe's Legacy of Caliban trilogy. He rides with the Second Company's elite outriders, serving in the secretive hunt for the Fallen.

A Dark Angels Space Marine of the Deathwing and Fifth Company, Telemenus serves as a POV character in Gav Thorpe's Legacy of Caliban trilogy. His arc spans the brutal campaigns tied to the Chapter's eternal hunt for the Fallen.

Master of the Ravenwing and Grand Master of the Second Company of the Dark Angels, Sammael leads the Chapter's fast-attack wing in the relentless pursuit of the Fallen. He rides the ancient jetbike Corvex and is a central figure in Gav Thorpe's Ravenwing novel.

High Interrogator-Chaplain of the Dark Angels, Sapphon is a senior keeper of the Chapter's most guarded secrets and a leading voice in the hunt for the Fallen. He features prominently in Gav Thorpe's Master of Sanctity.

Supreme Grand Master of the Dark Angels, Azrael bears the Lion Helm and is the Chapter's supreme commander and final authority over the Hunt for the Fallen. He is a central figure across Gav Thorpe's Legacy of Caliban series and the eponymous standalone novel.

Iskandar Khayon is an exiled Thousand Sons sorcerer who joined Abaddon's Black Legion after the Horus Heresy. He serves as the primary narrator and POV character of Aaron Dembski-Bowden's The Talon of Horus and Black Legion, recounting the Legion's founding from his own perspective.

Haldron-44 Stroika is a Skitarius Alpha of the Adeptus Mechanicus and the protagonist of Rob Sanders' Skitarius novel. He leads his maniple through brutal campaigns of arcane retrieval and technological war on behalf of the Machine God.

Omnid Torquora is a Tech-Priest Dominus of the Adeptus Mechanicus and superior officer to Haldron-44 Stroika in Rob Sanders' Skitarius and Tech-Priest novels. He directs the broader strategic mission that drives Stroika's campaigns.

Idriss Krendl is a Tech-Priest of the Adeptus Mechanicus appearing in Rob Sanders' Tech-Priest novel. A servant of the Omnissiah, he navigates the political and doctrinal tensions within the Machine Cult alongside the novel's broader cast.

Asurmen is the first and most ancient of the Eldar Phoenix Lords, known as the Hand of Asuryan and founder of the Dire Avengers Aspect. Gav Thorpe's eponymous novel spans millennia of his history, portraying him as both warrior and reluctant teacher of the Path of the Warrior.

O'Shovah, known as Commander Farsight, is a legendary T'au commander who broke from the T'au Empire to found the independent Farsight Enclaves. Phil Kelly's standalone novel and Crisis of Faith trilogy explore his centuries-long exile, his growing doubts about the T'au way, and his battles against the Orks and Chaos.

Koorland is the last surviving Imperial Fists Space Marine following the Ork assault on Ardamantua, who rises from Captain to Lord Commander of the Imperium during the catastrophic Beast Arises mega-series. His leadership shapes the desperate M32 Imperial response to the most dangerous Ork Waaagh! since the Great War.

Drakan Vangorich is the Grand Master of the Officio Assassinorum and a High Lord of Terra during the Age of the Beast. A central antagonist of the Beast Arises series, he manipulates Imperial politics with lethal precision and ultimately enacts the most audacious mass assassination in Imperial history.

The Beast is a colossal and uniquely intelligent Ork Warlord of M32 whose Waaagh! threatens to conquer the entire Imperium, driving the conflict at the heart of the Beast Arises series. Unlike any Ork before him, he can speak High Gothic and employs strategy alongside brute force.

Maximus Thane is the Chapter Master of the Fists Exemplar who succeeds Koorland as Lord Commander of the Imperium at the conclusion of the Beast Arises mega-series. He oversees the reconstitution of the Imperial Fists Legion successors into new Chapters following the war's devastating toll.

Inquisitorial Representative to the High Lords of Terra during the War of the Beast in M32, Marguerethe Wienand was among the first to recognise the severity of the Ork threat. A shrewd political operator, she navigated the fractious Senatorum Imperialis and ultimately shared the Representative role with rival Inquisitor Veritus.

Castellan of the Grey Knights Purifier Order, Garran Crowe is entrusted with the eternal burden of carrying the Blade of Antwyr — a daemon-bound sword too dangerous to be destroyed. He is the central figure of David Annandale's novels Warden of the Blade and Castellan.

A sentient sword housing a bound Slaaneshi daemon, the Blade of Antwyr is a relic so perilous that Grey Knight Castellan Garran Crowe is charged with carrying it so none weaker might fall to its whispers. It is a persistent voice of temptation and menace throughout Annandale's Warden of the Blade and Castellan.

Reaper Prime and commander of the Carcharodons Astra's 3rd Company, Bail Sharr leads the void-faring Space Marines in Robbie MacNiven's Red Tithe and Outer Dark. Stoic and relentless, he embodies the Chapter's predatory nature while shouldering the grim duty of securing new recruits across the stars.
