Scion of House Draconis on the Knight world of Adrastapol, Danial Tan Draconis begins Andy Clark's Kingsblade as an unblooded Knight Errant thrust into war against Chaos on Donatos. By Knightsblade he leads his world as High King, striving to unite Adrastapol's surviving noble houses.

Once an unblooded scion of House Chimaeros on Adrastapol, Luk became the Freeblade known as the Knight of Ashes after his house fell to Chaos heresy. Across Andy Clark's Kingsblade and Knightsblade he pursues his treacherous stepmother to reclaim his honour and avenge his kin.

Wolf Lord of the Drakeslayers Great Company and one of the Space Wolves' fiercest champions, Krom Dragongaze earned the epithet 'the Fierce-eye' for his formidable, headstrong nature. He wields the frost axe Wyrmclaw and features across the Space Wolves Limited Edition shorts cluster.

One of the most powerful Rune Priests in the Space Wolves Chapter, Njal Stormcaller commands the elemental fury of Fenris itself in battle. He is the eponymous subject of Chris Wraight's novel Stormcaller, which portrays his mastery of runic sorcery and his role defending his Chapter.

Second of the Eldar Phoenix Lords, Jain Zar is the Storm of Silence — supreme mistress of the Howling Banshees Aspect Warriors. Swift and merciless, she is among the most lethal warriors in the galaxy and is the subject of Gav Thorpe's novel Jain Zar: The Storm of Silence.

Guardian of the Lost and Death Company Chaplain of the Blood Angels, Lemartes is cursed with a particularly severe form of the Black Rage yet retains enough will to direct his berserk brethren in battle. He is the protagonist of David Annandale's novel Lemartes and appears in the Mephiston trilogy.

High King of Adrastapol and patriarch of House Draconis, Tolwyn Draconis leads the united Knight houses of his world into war against Chaos-corrupted Donatos in Andy Clark's Kingsblade. His fate during that campaign shapes the succession that drives the novel's central drama.

Consort of House Chimaeros and secret devotee of Tzeentch, Alicia Kar Manticos orchestrated the fall of her adopted house to Chaos on Adrastapol. She serves as the primary antagonist of Andy Clark's Knightsblade, hunted across the stars by her stepson Luk Kar Chimaeros.

Daughter of a xenoarchaeologist, Zelia Lor is the resourceful young protagonist of Cavan Scott's Warped Galaxies series — a Warhammer Adventures sequence aimed at younger readers. Separated from her mother after a Necron attack, she leads a small band of companions across the galaxy seeking safety.

A street-hardened Cadian boy and companion to Zelia Lor, Talen is the action-oriented heart of Cavan Scott's Warped Galaxies series. His scrappy, soldier-aspiring nature provides contrast to his more scholarly companions across the six-book Warhammer Adventures sequence.

A gifted Martian youth with an affinity for machines, Mekki serves as the tech-savvy companion in Cavan Scott's Warped Galaxies series. His Mechanicus-trained instincts and knack for improvised engineering frequently help the group survive across the five core novels of the series.

Fleapit is a small, loyal abhuman-like companion who travels with Zelia Lor and her friends in Cavan Scott's Warped Galaxies series. Part robot, part creature, Fleapit serves as a quirky but dependable ally across the children's adventure novels set in the 41st Millennium.

Minka Lesk is a Cadian Shock Trooper and one of the central protagonists of Justin D. Hill's Cadian Saga. A survivor of Cadia's fall, she rises through the ranks while fighting to preserve what remains of Cadian identity and military tradition across multiple brutal campaigns.

Erasmus Crowl is an Inquisitor of the Ordo Hereticus and the primary viewpoint character of Chris Wraight's Vaults of Terra trilogy. Operating in the shadow of Holy Terra itself, he investigates corruption and heresy at the very heart of the Imperium.

Luce Spinoza is an Inquisitorial Interrogator and the apprentice of Erasmus Crowl, featuring throughout Chris Wraight's Vaults of Terra trilogy. Her perspective provides a contrast to Crowl's veteran cynicism as she confronts the dark truths lurking beneath Terra's gilded surface.

Grekh is a Kroot tracker and mercenary who joins the ill-fated expedition into the Blackstone Fortress in Darius Hinks' novels. He serves as hunter and scout for the warband, bringing Kroot instincts and predatory skill to the dangers of the ancient alien structure.

Inquisitor Covenant is the driven protagonist of John French's Horusian Wars trilogy, an Inquisitor who pursues the dangerous theory that the power of the Horus Heresy can be turned to resurrect or empower the Imperium. His obsession courts heresy while battling genuine threats.

Janus Draik is a Rogue Trader and the leader of the expedition into the Blackstone Fortress in Darius Hinks' duology. Driven by ambition and a desire to unlock the fortress's secrets, he assembles a band of unlikely allies to venture into the deadly alien labyrinth.

Raus is one half of a pair of Ratling twins — alongside his brother Rein — who accompany Draik's expedition in Darius Hinks' Blackstone Fortress novels. The brothers provide stealth, sharpshooting, and survival instincts essential to navigating the fortress's lethal interior.

Rein is the other half of the Ratling twins (with Raus) who feature in Darius Hinks' Blackstone Fortress novels. Together, the brothers bring their abhuman agility and sharpshooting talent to Draik's perilous mission inside the xenos structure.

Aleksei Lev Tieron is the Chancellor of the Senatorum Imperialis and one of the High Twelve in Chris Wraight's Watchers of the Throne duology. He navigates the treacherous politics of the Imperium's ruling council during the era of the Regent's shadow government.

Audus is a pilot and retainer serving Rogue Trader Janus Draik in Darius Hinks' Blackstone Fortress novels. She provides essential crew support and aerial skill to the expedition, though her relationship with Draik is marked by tension and guarded loyalty.

Harleen Amity is an Inquisitor who appears in Cavan Scott's Warped Galaxies children's series, notably in the Tau and Orks volumes. She pursues the young protagonists across the galaxy, representing the Inquisition's ruthless reach even in a younger-audience setting.

Isola is a factotum and seneschal in the employ of Rogue Trader Janus Draik, appearing in Darius Hinks' Blackstone Fortress novels. She handles logistics and intelligence for the expedition, serving as the practical backbone of Draik's ambitious undertaking.

Taddeus is a zealous Ecclesiarchy preacher who joins Draik's expedition into the Blackstone Fortress in Darius Hinks' novels. His fanatical faith drives him to see the xenos fortress as a site of sacred significance, bringing firebrand devotion to the warband's mission.

Tanau Aleya is a Sister of Silence and Vigilator paired with the Custodian Valerian across Chris Wraight's Watchers of the Throne duology. Her null-soul aura and warrior discipline complement Valerian's strength as they guard Terra against existential threats during the Time of Ending.

Tribune of the Adeptus Custodes, Valerian serves as one of the Emperor's immortal guardians during the tumultuous era following the Siege of Terra. Paired with Sister of Silence Tanau Aleya, he is a central figure in Chris Wraight's Watchers of the Throne duology.

Pious Vorne is a fanatical Ministorum zealot and flamer-wielding retainer in Janus Draik's expedition party. She appears across Darius Hinks' Blackstone Fortress duology, providing both firepower and religious fervour to the desperate delves into the ancient fortress.

Emissary of Ynnead and prophet of the Ynnari, Yvraine rose from the arenas of Commorragh to become the spiritual leader of eldar seeking salvation through their god of the dead. She is a central figure in the unification of the scattered eldar factions and features across Gav Thorpe's Ynnari novels.

Chapter Master of the Flesh Tearers, Gabriel Seth leads one of the most bloodthirsty Blood Angels successor chapters, struggling to maintain control over his warriors' savage Red Thirst. He plays a key role in the defence of Baal in Guy Haley's Devastation of Baal.

High Consul of the Iron Council and de facto leader of the Iron Hands Chapter, Kardan Stronos embodies his Chapter's doctrine of cold logic over emotional weakness. He is the central figure of David Guymer's Iron Hands trilogy, navigating the brutal politics of Medusa and the wider war for survival.

The Sword of Ynnead, the Visarch serves as Yvraine's deadly bodyguard and enforcer within the Ynnari. His identity is shrouded in mystery. He forms one third of the Ynnari triumvirate alongside Yvraine and the Yncarne, appearing in Gav Thorpe's Ghost Warrior.

The Avatar of Ynnead, the Yncarne is a living embodiment of the eldar god of the dead, manifested from the souls of the fallen. As the third member of the Ynnari triumvirate, it acts as a powerful instrument of Ynnead's will, appearing in Gav Thorpe's Ghost Warrior.

An Iyanden Spiritseer of great renown, Iyanna Arienal communes with the dead warriors bound within Wraithguard and Wraithlords to fight for the craftworld. In Gav Thorpe's Ghost Warrior she joins forces with Yvraine's Ynnari in a search for the lost craftworld Ziasuthra.

Probator Agusto Zidarov is a weary, middle-aged enforcer serving in the rain-drenched hive city of Varangantua. He is the POV protagonist of Chris Wraight's Bloodlines, the first Warhammer Crime novel, tasked with finding a missing aristocratic heir while navigating gang warfare and corporate corruption.

A Necron noble of the Ithakas dynasty and brother to the exiled Nemesor Oltyx, Djoseras is principled and dutiful where his sibling is rebellious. He features across Nate Crowley's Twice-Dead King trilogy as a foil and sometimes-ally to Oltyx during the dynasty's struggle for survival.

Nemesor Oltyx is the exiled Necron lord of the Ithakas dynasty and protagonist of Nate Crowley's Twice-Dead King trilogy. Stripped of his rightful place through court scheming and banished to a forgotten world, he must fight to preserve both his dynasty and what remains of his fractured consciousness.

A Necron Cryptek vizier serving Nemesor Oltyx in the Ithakas dynasty, Yenekh is both a skilled warrior and fleet commander. He appears throughout Nate Crowley's Twice-Dead King trilogy, fighting a losing personal battle against the Flayer Curse while remaining loyal to Oltyx.

Brother-Sergeant of Damocles Squad and a veteran of the Iron Snakes Chapter, Priad is a formidable Space Marine warrior with a long history of honourable service. In Matthew Farrer's Urdesh duology he leads his squad in defence of the reborn Saint Sabbat on the forge world of Urdesh.

A fast-talking Ratling sniper and petty criminal at large in the hive city of Varangantua, Baggit is the scheming half of the Baggit-and-Clodde duo. He stars in Alec Worley's audio drama Dredge Runners and the novel The Wraithbone Phoenix, always chasing the next score.

An Ogryn deserter with a gentle nature beneath his immense frame, Clodde is the loyal muscle of the Baggit-and-Clodde pair. He appears in Alec Worley's Dredge Runners and The Wraithbone Phoenix, navigating the criminal underworld of Varangantua with more decency than his partner.

A ruthless Warhammer Crime-era crime lord who serves as the primary antagonist in Alec Worley's Dredge Runners. Kruger is a vicious underworld fixer whose deal with the ratling Baggit and ogryn Clodde goes badly wrong, setting the duology in motion.

Leonid Rostov is an Ordo Hereticus Inquisitor with potent psychic gifts, trained under Inquisitor Lord Jeren Dyre. He appears in the Dawn of Fire series pursuing the Hand of Abaddon and investigating miraculous visions threatening Imperial stability.

Ferren Areios is a Captain of the Ultramarines 6th Company, elevated from the Unnumbered Sons by Roboute Guilliman himself. He is a prominent figure across Nick Kyme's Dawn of Fire entries and received his own spin-off novel, Master of Rites.

Solomon Akurra is the Harrowmaster of the Alpha Legion warband the Serpent's Teeth, determined to unite the Legion's fractured warbands and continue the Long War during the Indomitus Crusade. He is the POV protagonist of Mike Brooks' Renegades duology.

Nemesor Zahndrekh is a Sautekh Dynasty Necron lord whose mind became fractured during the Great Sleep, leaving him convinced he still fights the wars of his mortal era. His eccentric perception of battle and unshakeable nobility make him a singular figure among the Necrons, explored in Nate Crowley's Severed.

Vargard Obyron is the fiercely loyal bodyguard of Nemesor Zahndrekh, bound to protect his lord regardless of the eccentric commands he receives. He appears alongside Zahndrekh in Nate Crowley's Severed, a key figure in the Sautekh Dynasty's fiction.

Trajann Valoris is the Captain-General of the Adeptus Custodes, the supreme commander of the Emperor's personal guard and one of the most powerful warriors in the Imperium. He features in Nick Kyme's Auric Gods and is a major lore figure of the Indomitus era.

Kor Phaeron is the Master of the Faith of the Word Bearers, one of Lorgar's most devoted sons and a central architect of the Horus Heresy alongside his rival Erebus. A former mortal raised to Astartes status by Lorgar himself, he appears in Guy Haley's Throne of Light.

Makari is Ghazghkull Thraka's legendary grot banner-bearer, known among the Orks as Da Lucky 'Un for his seemingly supernatural ability to survive catastrophe. He is the unlikely narrator of Nate Crowley's Ghazghkull Thraka: Prophet of the Waaagh!, recounting his master's life to an Inquisitor.

Inquisitor Falx is a radical Ordo Xenos Inquisitor who serves as the framing device of Nate Crowley's Ghazghkull Thraka: Prophet of the Waaagh!, interrogating the grot Makari to piece together the warlord's biography. Her unusual methods and retinue reflect her radical outlook.

Iron Queen Orlah rules the Knight world of Kamidar at the head of House Kamidar, a proud and independent realm that clashes with the arriving Indomitus Crusade fleet in Nick Kyme's The Iron Kingdom. Her resistance to Imperial demands drives the novel's central conflict.

Garviel Loken is a Captain of the Luna Wolves and member of the Mournival, Horus's inner council of advisors. He serves as the primary moral conscience and POV character of the Horus Heresy's opening trilogy, witnessing the Legion's fall from within.

Tarik Torgaddon is a Luna Wolves Captain and Mournival member, known for his irreverent humour and unshakeable personal honour. He stands alongside Loken in the opening Horus Heresy trilogy as one of the Legion's most loyal sons.

Horus Aximand, nicknamed 'Little Horus' for his physical resemblance to the Warmaster, is a Sons of Horus Mournival captain who struggles with identity and loyalty as the Heresy unfolds. He appears across the opening trilogy and multiple later Horus Heresy anthologies.

Erebus is the First Chaplain of the Word Bearers and the principal instigator of the Horus Heresy, manipulating events from behind the scenes to corrupt Horus and ignite civil war. He is one of the most consequential villains across the Heresy series, featuring most prominently in The First Heretic.

Battle-Captain of the Death Guard's Seventh Great Company and one of the Legion's staunchest loyalists. Escaping the Isstvan III massacre aboard the frigate Eisenstein, he carries word of Horus's treachery to Terra. He later becomes a founding agent of Malcador's Knights-Errant.

First Lord of Terra and Regent of the Imperium, second in authority only to the Emperor himself during the Great Crusade and Heresy. Architect of many clandestine Imperial institutions, including the Knights-Errant and the forerunner of the Inquisition.

Dark Angels Astartes who rises to serve as a Chaplain during the Legion's turbulent pre-Heresy years on Caliban. A cousin and close foil to Zahariel, his path through the Order and the Legion is chronicled across the Descent of Angels and Fallen Angels novels.

Dark Angels Librarian whose arc spans Caliban, the Order, and the Legion's growing schism. A central POV in Descent of Angels and Fallen Angels, he grapples with psychic power and the shadow that Luther's rebellion casts over the First Legion.

Captain-General of the Adeptus Custodes throughout the Great Crusade and Horus Heresy, the most senior guardian of the Emperor's person. Renowned as peerless even among the Custodian Guard, he stands as one of the most formidable warriors in Imperial history.

Captain of the Emperor's Children's Tenth Company and a rare loyalist within a Legion sliding into excess. He defied his treacherous superiors at Isstvan III, warning his fellow loyalists of the incoming virus-bombing and leading their doomed last stand on the surface.

Word Bearers Captain and commander of the Serrated Sun Chapter, he is among the first Astartes to be possessed by a daemon through the Gal Vorbak ritual on Cadia. The primary POV of The First Heretic, his descent into daemonhood shapes the Word Bearers' path to open rebellion.

Veteran Luna Wolves/Sons of Horus Captain known as 'The Half-heard' for his old-fashioned counsel that few chose to listen to. One of the few loyalists within Horus's own Legion, he sided with Garro and eventually became one of Malcador's Knights-Errant.

Terran scholar and skjald (remembrancer) embedded with the Space Wolves Legion, whose investigation of a massacre on Shrike becomes entangled with his own hidden past. The mortal POV of Prospero Burns, he unwittingly serves as a Cabal asset placed near Leman Russ.

Remembrancer assigned to the Luna Wolves during the Great Crusade, serving as a documentary witness and key POV character in Horus Rising. Her chronicles of the Legion and her close relationship with Loken give a mortal perspective on the unfolding tragedy of the Heresy.

Iterator attached to the Luna Wolves Expeditionary Fleet, tasked with spreading the Imperial Truth to newly compliant worlds. A scholarly mortal POV in Horus Rising, his faith in the secular Imperium is shaken as the seeds of Chaos take root around him.

An immortal human operative serving the alien Cabal, whose long centuries of service have given him mastery of many languages and identities. The POV of Legion, he is tasked with subverting an Alpha Legion primarch in the Cabal's scheme to end the Heresy on their own terms.

Fabricator-General of Mars, the supreme political and spiritual leader of the Mechanicum at the time of the Heresy. He allied with Horus and unleashed the Kaban Project's war-machines, triggering the Schism of Mars and delivering the forge-world's vast resources to the Warmaster.

Lord Commander of the Emperor's Children and one of Fulgrim's most senior officers, notable for his arrogance and martial ambition. He features prominently in Fulgrim as the Legion embraces Slaanesh, and later reappears across subsequent Heresy and 41st Millennium novels.

First Captain of the Emperor's Children and a formidable warrior within Fulgrim's inner circle. Present at Isstvan III and a key figure in Fulgrim, he embodies the Legion's slide into excess as Slaanesh's corruption takes hold during the early Heresy campaigns.

Raven Guard captain and commander of the Raptors, the Legion's covert-operations wing. He serves as a key loyalist figure during the siege of Deliverance, helping Corax organise the Legion's survival after Isstvan V.

Death Guard Legionary and sergeant who becomes the first of Mortarion's warriors to succumb to the supernatural plague aboard the Eisenstein. His transformation marks an early, pivotal moment in the Legion's corruption.

Housecarl and personal attendant to Death Guard Captain Nathaniel Garro aboard the Eisenstein. A mortal servant who witnesses the supernatural horror unfolding among the Legion and stands alongside Garro during the desperate flight to Terra.

Hetman of the Geno Five-Two Chiliad, an elite Imperial Army regiment deployed during the Crusade-era compliance of 670. A veteran commander whose loyalist perspective forms a central viewpoint in Dan Abnett's Legion.

Geno Five-Two Chiliad officer serving alongside Hurtado Bronzi during the occupation of 670. One of the primary point-of-view characters in Legion, drawn into the mystery surrounding the Alpha Legion's true allegiances.

Senior officer of the Geno Five-Two Chiliad and comrade to Bronzi and Soneka. He plays a supporting command role during the events of Legion as the Chiliad's loyalties and mission are tested by Alpha Legion manipulation.

Knight of the Order of Caliban, the proto-Space Marines warrior brotherhood that predates the Dark Angels Legion. He features in Descent of Angels as one of the Order's seasoned fighters navigating Caliban's transformation under the Lion.

Ultramarines captain and primary loyalist protagonist of Battle for the Abyss. He leads a desperate pursuit to intercept the Word Bearers' Furious Abyss before the vessel can unleash destruction upon Macragge.

Space Wolves Wolf Guard warrior and one of the loyalist heroes aboard the intercepting strike force in Battle for the Abyss. His ferocity and honour are defining traits as he fights alongside Astartes of other Legions against the Word Bearers threat.

World Eaters Legionary serving among the loyalist interceptors in Battle for the Abyss. A fierce and aggressive warrior, he represents Angron's Legion before their full descent, fighting alongside brothers from rival Legions against the Furious Abyss.

Thousand Sons Astartes and psyker who joins the loyalist force pursuing the Furious Abyss in Battle for the Abyss. His sorcerous gifts prove invaluable but also a source of tension among his more suspicious battle-brothers.

Word Bearers captain and commander of the Furious Abyss, the massive warship at the heart of Battle for the Abyss. A fanatical servant of Lorgar, he drives a devastating strike against the Ultramarines' home system.

Custodian Guard assigned as the Emperor's observer with the Word Bearers Legion in The First Heretic. His presence among Lorgar's warriors reflects the Emperor's distrust, and his fate underscores the depth of the Word Bearers' fall.

Imperial Remembrancer and one of the civilian observers embedded with the Thousand Sons on Prospero. In A Thousand Sons, his friendship with Ahriman gives a mortal's-eye view of the Legion's eventual fate during the Burning of Prospero.

Imperial Astropath and the central protagonist of The Outcast Dead, stranded on Terra in the days after Isstvan V. Haunted by a catastrophic message he received, he becomes entangled with a group of renegade Space Marines outside the Walls of the Palace.

A former Luna Wolves Astartes who became one of the so-called Outcast Dead following the Edict of Nikaea. Branded an apostate and psyker, he escapes from the Custodian-guarded prison aboard Terra alongside a small band of condemned legionaries.

A World Eaters Space Marine psyker imprisoned beneath the Petitioner's City on Terra. One of the Outcast Dead, Tagore is among the condemned legionaries whose desperate escape forms the core of Graham McNeill's novel of the same name.

A Thousand Sons psyker Astartes, one of the Outcast Dead imprisoned on Terra after the Edict of Nikaea outlawed Space Marine sorcery. His formidable psychic gifts make him the most dangerous and pivotal member of the condemned fugitive band.

Ultramarines Captain and one of the primary POV characters in Dan Abnett's Know No Fear. Stationed on Calth when the Word Bearers launch their surprise assault, Ventanus rallies the shattered XIII Legion and becomes a linchpin of the desperate defence.

A Vindicare Temple assassin and one of two lead characters in James Swallow's Nemesis. Kell is dispatched as part of a Clade coalition assembled to eliminate Horus, representing the pinnacle of long-range precision killing within the Officio Assassinorum.

A terrifying Chaos-aligned assassin and the primary antagonist of James Swallow's Nemesis. Spear is a warp-gifted killer sent by the powers of Chaos to slay the Emperor, hunting across Terra while a rival Imperial kill-team closes in on Horus.

An Adeptus Arbites investigator serving as a mortal POV character in Nemesis. Sabrat works to unravel a web of conspiracy on Dagonet as the Heresy's shadow falls across the Imperium, grounding the novel in the perspective of an ordinary citizen-officer.

A Mechanicum adept appearing in Graham McNeill's Mechanicum. Caxton is a supporting figure within the factional politics of the red planet as Mars fractures between loyalty and the emerging Schism that will tear the Mechanicum apart.

The central human POV of Graham McNeill's Mechanicum, a gifted but unaugmented tech-savant brought to Mars to work on a secret project beneath the Noctis Labyrinthus. Her investigation into the Akashic Reader brings her to the heart of the Martian civil war.

A senior Mechanicum Adept and emissary to the Sons of Horus in Graham McNeill's Mechanicum. Regulus represents the Martian faction that has thrown its allegiance behind Horus, facilitating the planet-wide Schism that devastates the forge world.

Magos and mistress of the Magma City forge on Mars, one of the most powerful Mechanicum figures in Graham McNeill's Mechanicum. Zeth remains staunchly loyal to the Emperor and fights to preserve the Noctis Labyrinthus project against the Schismatic uprising.

A Sister of Silence of the Witchseeker cadre, appearing in the anthology Tales of Heresy. Kendel is a blank, deadly to psykers, and her story explores the grim work of the silent sisterhood in the earliest days of the Horus Heresy.

An Imperial cult priest and mortal POV character in the Tales of Heresy anthology. Olathaire's story examines faith and doubt within the Emperor's secular Imperium, positioning him at the collision point between the outlawed religion and official Imperial ideology.

A White Scars brotherband warrior and recurring POV across Chris Wraight's Heresy arc — Scars, Path of Heaven, and Warhawk. Shiban embodies the legion's fierce independence and plays a central role in the White Scars' long-deferred choice of allegiance.

Salamanders captain and equerry to Primarch Vulkan, central to Nick Kyme's Vulkan Lives, Deathfire, and Born of Flame. Numeon bears the crushing burden of his primarch's apparent death and drives the Salamanders' desperate quest to recover Vulkan.

A former remembrancer embedded with the Luna Wolves, Euphrati Keeler became one of the earliest and most prominent figures in the Lectitio Divinitatus cult, which would evolve into the Imperial Creed. Her apparent ability to repel daemonic forces cemented her status as a living saint.

One of the Perpetuals — immortal humans who have existed since before recorded history — Oll Persson is an old soldier who has fought in countless wars across the millennia. His wandering path eventually leads him toward Terra itself during the closing days of the Heresy.

First Captain of the Imperial Fists during the Horus Heresy, Sigismund was renowned as the greatest swordsman in the Legiones Astartes. His unwavering faith in the Emperor led him to found the Black Templars Chapter after the Heresy's end.

An Ultramarines sergeant marked with red helm-trim as censure for advocating combat-readiness against Space Marines, Thiel proved his philosophy correct at the Battle of Calth. His tactical instincts made him a key figure in the Ultramarines' survival against the Word Bearers' treachery.

A tech-archaeologist of the Mechanicum famed for rediscovering the Land Raider and Land Speeder patterns that bear his name, Arkhan Land served the Emperor directly during the War Within the Webway. Eccentric and brilliant, he is accompanied by his pet psyber-monkey.

A Space Wolves warrior who served under Leman Russ during the Horus Heresy, Bjorn survived the Primarch's departure into the Eye of Terror. He would go on to become Bjorn the Fell-Handed, the oldest surviving Space Marine and a revered Dreadnought of the Space Wolves.

A towering Bloodthirster of Khorne and the enduring nemesis of Primarch Sanguinius, Ka'Bandha clashed with the Angel on Signus Prime and again at the Siege of Terra. Their enmity is one of the defining conflicts of the Horus Heresy's daemonic narrative.

A mortal Imperial Navy officer who commanded the World Eaters flagship Conqueror, Lotara Sarrin earned the respect of Angron's Legion through sheer competence and nerve. Her tenure aboard the ship witnessed the progressive descent of the World Eaters into unstoppable savagery.

First Captain of the Night Lords and leader of the elite Atramentar Terminators, Sevatar was known as the 'Prince of Crows' and feared even among his own treacherous Legion. His sardonic wit and lethal skill made him one of the Horus Heresy's most memorable villains.

An Iron Hands captain and one of the Shattered Legions survivors of Isstvan V, Shadrak Meduson forged a guerrilla coalition of loyalist Legions to wage war behind enemy lines. His relentless campaign against the Sons of Horus made him one of Horus's most hunted enemies.

A Sons of Horus captain known as 'The Either', Tybalt Marr is a calculating and devoted servant of Horus tasked with hunting down the Shattered Legions survivors. He serves as a primary antagonist in the post-Isstvan guerrilla war arc of the Horus Heresy series.

Originally a Codicier of the Ultramarines, Tylos Rubio was one of the first Space Marines recruited by Nathaniel Garro into the nascent Knights-Errant. His psyker abilities — suppressed under the Nikaea edict — became a vital asset for the proto-Inquisition's secret work.

A Word Bearers Crimson Lord and master of dark sorcery, Zardu Layak bound twin Possessed warriors as his personal guard. He served as a key figure in the Word Bearers' daemonic operations during the later stages of the Horus Heresy, wielding considerable influence within the Legion's inner circle.

A White Scars Stormseer (psyker-shaman) and one of Jaghatai Khan's most trusted advisors, Targutai Yesugei served as a voice of wisdom during the Legion's long isolation and eventual commitment to the loyalist cause. His death is one of the defining sacrifices of the White Scars' Heresy arc.

Captain of the Sanguinary Guard and among Sanguinius's most devoted protectors, Azkaellon stood at his Primarch's side during the Siege of Terra. He endured the grief of failing to prevent the Angel's death and took on the burden of preserving the Blood Angels' honour in the aftermath.

First Captain of the Blood Angels during the Horus Heresy, commanding the Legion's elite First Company. Raldoron plays a pivotal leadership role in the Signus Prime campaign depicted in *Fear to Tread*, holding the Legion together in the face of daemonic corruption.

A Blood Angels Apothecary who serves a crucial role during the Signus Prime campaign in *Fear to Tread*. His actions during the Legion's desperate struggle against daemonic forces on Signus define his arc as one of the novel's central characters.

A Blood Angels Space Marine who features in the Horus Heresy novel *Bringer of Sorrow*. Zephon is notable for his presence in the Siege of Terra arc, serving as a warrior of the IXth Legion during the final years of the Heresy.

A Captain of the Imperial Fists renowned for his extraordinary defensive instincts and tactical brilliance. Polux survives the devastating Iron Cage engagement and later becomes the founder and first Chapter Master of the Crimson Fists Space Marine Chapter.

Master of the Huscarls, the personal honour guard of Primarch Rogal Dorn, during the Horus Heresy. Archamus serves as a central POV character in *Praetorian of Dorn*, embodying the Imperial Fists' duty and loyalty during the defence of the Solar System.

A Thousand Sons captain who appears in *The Crimson King*, serving Magnus the Red during the tumultuous period following the Burning of Prospero. He operates within the fractured remnants of the XVth Legion as they pursue their Primarch's agenda.

A Thousand Sons Space Marine featuring in *The Crimson King*, part of the Legion's surviving forces following the destruction of Prospero. He serves Magnus the Red during the XVth Legion's exile and continued pursuit of arcane knowledge.

A Thousand Sons warrior who appears in *The Crimson King*, among the Legion's survivors after the Fall of Prospero. He is one of the XVth Legion's gifted psykers continuing to serve Magnus as the Horus Heresy progresses.

Captain of the Pavoni Fellowship, one of the Thousand Sons' psychic cults, during the Horus Heresy. Hathor Maat is notable for his mastery of biomantic sorcery and features in *The Crimson King* among Magnus's surviving inner circle.

Ancient of the Emperor's Children, an honoured warrior interred within a Dreadnought sarcophagus, who refuses to follow his Legion into depravity. He becomes a pivotal figure in *Angel Exterminatus*, acting against Fulgrim's schemes on the world of Iydris.

Equerry to Warmaster Horus and a former member of the Mournival, bearing the epithet 'the Twisted' after catastrophic injuries sustained in battle. Maloghurst serves as Horus's closest political advisor and administrator throughout the Heresy, appearing prominently in *Slaves to Darkness*.

A powerful daemon known as the 'Lord of Hosts' who serves as the primary antagonist of the Signus Prime campaign in *Fear to Tread*. Madail commands the daemonic forces that ambush the Blood Angels Legion, testing Sanguinius and his warriors to their breaking point.

An Iron Hands Space Marine who serves as a key POV character in *The Damnation of Pythos*, stranded with a remnant force on the death world of Pythos following the Dropsite Massacre at Isstvan V. His story explores survivor's guilt and the slow horror of that isolated campaign.

A Salamanders Space Marine and survivor of the Isstvan V Dropsite Massacre, serving with the mixed remnant force stranded on the death world of Pythos. He is a central character in *The Damnation of Pythos*, representing the XVIIIth Legion's resilience amid growing despair.

A Word Bearers Space Marine who turns against his own Legion, aligning himself with the Iron Hands in pursuit of a mission to destroy the Fulgurite, a relic of the Emperor. Narek appears in *Old Earth* as a conflicted figure whose anti-Chaos agenda sets him apart from his traitorous brothers.

A Perpetual operative loyal to the Emperor, Alivia Sureka is one of the rare immortal humans woven into the Heresy's hidden threads. Her story runs parallel to Oll Persson's across the Old Wounds, New Scars anthology, grounding the Perpetual thread in a civilian perspective.

An ordinary Imperial Army soldier, Katsuhiro serves as a ground-level POV character during the Siege of Terra in The Lost and the Damned. His narrative anchors the human cost of the siege amid the titanic clash of Legiones Astartes.

A Knight-Centura of the Sisters of Silence, Mohana Mankata Vi commands a cadre of the Emperor's null-maidens during the Titandeath campaign over Beta-Garmon. Her presence anchors the Sisters of Silence's role in the war for the Solar approaches.

Princeps Senior of Legio Solaria, Esha Ani Mohana leads her all-female Titan Legion through the devastating Beta-Garmon campaign depicted in Titandeath. She is a central figure in the Mechanicum's desperate defence of the Solar approaches against traitor Titan forces.

A Word Bearers Dark Apostle, Kurtha Sedd wages an unrelenting war of attrition against loyalist survivors beneath the irradiated surface of Calth in The Honoured and The Unburdened. He embodies the Word Bearers' fanatical drive to exterminate the Ultramarines to the last.

An Ultramarines commander and namesake of the novella The Honoured, Steloc Aethon leads the loyalist remnants fighting the Calth Underworld War. His dogged resistance against Kurtha Sedd's Word Bearers defines the guerrilla struggle in the tunnels beneath Calth.

A loyalist Iron Warriors Warsmith, Barabas Dantioch refused Perturabo's treachery and fought on the side of the Emperor throughout the Heresy. Notable across the Hydra Cordatus arc and Perturabo: The Hammer of Olympia, he stands as the lone-loyalist archetype within his Legion.

A Word Bearers commander, Sor Talgron leads the Legion's assault on Percepton Primus in The Purge. His campaign is an act of deliberate massacre, embodying the Word Bearers' systematic erasure of Imperial worlds that refused to embrace Chaos.

A daemonic entity encountered in the Eye of Terror, Ingethel serves as Lorgar's guide through the Warp in the novella Aurelian. Her revelations to the Urizen sealed the Word Bearers' fall to Chaos and proved pivotal to the entire Horus Heresy.

Uwoma Kandawire served as Grand Provost Marshal during the Wars of Unification and is a central antagonist in Valdor: Birth of the Imperium. Suspicious of the Thunder Warriors' fate and the rise of a new secret army, she engages in a tense power struggle against Constantin Valdor himself.

A genewright active during the Unification era, Amar Astarte is tied to the secret programme producing the Emperor's new transhuman warriors in Valdor: Birth of the Imperium. Her work stands at the shadowed origins of the Space Marine Legions.

A Departmento Munitorum liaison attached to the White Scars, Ilya Ravallion appears across Brotherhood of the Storm and Path of Heaven as a rare human perspective within the V Legion. Her loyalty to the Khan and her insight into Legion politics make her a recurring thread in the White Scars arc.

A senior White Scars commander and Noyan-Khan, Hasik led the Horde of the Stone during the Great Crusade. Featured in Jaghatai Khan: Warhawk of Chogoris, he is notable for his pro-Horus sympathies and involvement in an attempted coup against the Khan's loyalist course.

Voted Lieutenant of the Deathwing, Holguin is a senior Dark Angels officer featured in the Dreadwing novel. One of the Legion's inner circle, he operates within the secretive Hexagrammaton at the height of the Horus Heresy.

Farith Redloss served as Voted Lieutenant and Dreadbringer of the Dark Angels' Dreadwing, the Legion's wing specialising in weapons of mass destruction. Featured in the Dreadwing novel, he later became Supreme Grand Master of the Dark Angels in the aftermath of the Heresy.

Iron Warriors officer and antagonist during the Tallarn campaign. He commands armoured forces in the brutal war of attrition fought across Tallarn's underground galleries and poisoned surface, earning a reputation for methodical siege doctrine.

The Chaos God of disease, decay, and entropy, called Grandfather Nurgle by his followers. He welcomed Mortarion into his pestilential Garden, and his patronage over Death Guard and mortal cultists alike makes him one of the four great powers of the Warp.

Thousand Sons psyker who survived the Burning of Prospero and later fought alongside the Shattered Legions. His story reaches its defining moment in the ruins of Tizca, where the legacy of Magnus's sons weighs on every survivor.

Therion Cohort officer attached to the Raven Guard during the Horus Heresy. He serves as an Imperial Army liaison figure across the Beta-Garmon theatre and the campaigns under Corax, representing the mortal soldiery bound to Astartes command.

Ultramarine officer serving as an ambassador and cross-arc representative of the XIIIth Legion during the Heresy. He appears on Bastion alongside Salamander emissary Heka'tan and later in events framed around the approach to Terra.

Iterator and remembrancer present during the Heresy era, best known for a charged confrontation with Rogal Dorn and Iacton Qruze on Titan regarding the ethics of execution. His case stands as a foundational moment for the civilian remembrancer arc of the Sons of Horus narrative.

Dark Angels Voted Lieutenant and one of the Lion's closest lieutenants during the Heresy. He participates in the tense parlay against Konrad Curze at Tsagualsa alongside Lion El'Jonson, establishing himself as a key figure in the First Legion's later campaigns.

Former Mournival captain of the Luna Wolves, killed before the Heresy began. His identity was later exploited by Erebus's cult through an impersonator, a deception that forms a foundational and disturbing beat in the mythology surrounding Horus's fall.

Iron Hands captain stationed on Terra as part of the Crusader Host during the Heresy. He is a central figure in the Riven novella, navigating the tensions between surviving Xth Legion warriors and their Imperial Fists hosts in the shadow of the Siege.

Thousand Sons psyker and survivor of Prospero who confronts Khârn amid the ruins of Tizca, closing a key thread of the Burning of Prospero arc. He appears alongside Revuel Arvida in the novella Rebirth.

Salamanders Space Marine serving as his Legion's ambassador on the world of Bastion. He stands alongside Ultramarine envoy Arcadese in Forgotten Sons, embodying the Salamanders' role as a moral compass among the Legions during the Heresy.

World Eaters captain present at the pivotal confrontation on Desh'ea, where Khârn faces the newly primarch-awakened Angron. He is part of the War Hounds leadership navigating the violent transformation of the Legion into the World Eaters.

Mechanicum adept and POV character in The Kaban Project, a prequel novella to Mechanicum. She works alongside figures tied to the Sons of Horus and Lukas Chrom, providing a Martian perspective on early Heresy-era machinations.

Alpha Legion operative and propagandist deployed on Virger-Mos II, whose name — Latin for 'we lie' — reflects the Legion's ethos. He is the primary Astartes agent in Liar's Due, manipulating civilian targets through carefully engineered disinformation.

Raven Guard Shadowmaster renowned for exceptional stealth and lethality, first introduced alongside Iron Hand Sabik Wayland in Kryptos. He becomes a key member of the Sisypheum crew and recurs as one of the Shattered Legions' most formidable warriors through Angel Exterminatus and beyond.

Iron Hands Techmarine and survivor of the Isstvan V Dropsite Massacre who served aboard the renegade vessel Sisypheum. He fought alongside Nykona Sharrowkyn and a band of Legion survivors waging a guerrilla war against the Traitor forces during the Heresy.

Imperial Army officer serving as governor-militant on Tallarn in the aftermath of the devastating virus-bombing and subsequent tank war. She surveyed the ruined wasteland from a Titan and bore witness to the catastrophic cost of the conflict.

A Night Lords Legionary who once bore the forbidden rank of Librarian before the Council of Nikaea outlawed psychic warfare. His story is a character study of guilt and identity as he grapples with his suppressed abilities and loyalty to a Legion defined by terror.

Former World Eaters captain who survived the Isstvan III betrayal and later joined the Knights Errant under Malcador the Sigillite. He appears across multiple Horus Heresy audio dramas alongside Nathaniel Garro, serving as a lethal and volatile asset in covert operations.

Knights Errant operative and trusted field agent who worked closely with Nathaniel Garro on multiple covert missions during the Horus Heresy. He confronted Alpha Legion infiltrators in the Jovian shipyards alongside fellow agent Amendera Kendel.

Female pariah-princeps of the Ordo Sinister, the secretive order of Psi-Titans tasked with defending the Imperial Webway project. She is the protagonist of the audio drama bearing the order's name, piloting a Warlord Titan whose null-aura suppresses the warp.

Astropath and co-protagonist of the audio drama featuring Malcador the Sigillite, where she serves as an interlocutor and witness to the First Lord of the Imperium's reflections on the war and his role in the Emperor's great work.

A renegade Space Marine who cast aside his Legion identity to fight as a Blackshield during the Horus Heresy. He is the earliest named Blackshield viewpoint character, introduced in the short story bearing the movement's name.

Imperial Fists Librarian recruited into the Knights Errant by Nathaniel Garro during a mission aboard Rogal Dorn's flagship the Phalanx. His induction represents the growing network of agents Malcador was assembling in secret for the long war ahead.

Word Bearers Chaplain or sorcerer responsible for constructing the Templum Daemonarchia on the world of Kronus during the Shadow Crusade era. His work exemplifies the Legion's drive to systematise daemonic worship and bind warp entities to the Traitor cause.

Former Iron Hands captain who turned renegade and led a warband of Blackshields — warriors who renounced their Legion allegiances — through three linked campaigns during the latter Horus Heresy. His trilogy by Josh Reynolds charts the Blackshields as a distinct fighting force.

Mortal agent of the Officio Sigillite and close operative of Malcador, active on Terra and in the ancient lands of Gyptus during the Horus Heresy. He serves as deuteragonist alongside Malcador in the audio drama bearing the Sigillite's title.

Sons of Horus Space Marine who served as a personal emissary of Horus Lupercal during the latter stages of the Heresy. He carried his primarch's will to conquered worlds, overseeing the submission of planetary populations to the new order.

Imperial agent working under Malcador the Sigillite who was tasked with hunting down the fugitive Space Wolf Severian during the Horus Heresy. He is the protagonist of the audio drama Wolf Hunt by Graham McNeill.

Thousand Sons sorcerer and survivor of the Burning of Prospero who retreated with Magnus the Red to the Planet of Sorcerers within the Eye of Terror. He appears alongside Ahriman and other Legion survivors as they confront the consequences of their fall.

Heresy-era Thousand Sons Space Marine and Equerry to Primarch Magnus the Red. Amon led the Corvidae cult of precognitives as its magister templi and captain of the 9th Fellowship, having first served as Magnus's tutor on Prospero. He later joined Ahriman's cabal before vanishing from record.

Loyalist Forge Master and Fabricator Locum of Mars during the Horus Heresy, Kane refused to follow Kelbor-Hal into the Dark Mechanicum. He led loyalist resistance through the Schism of Mars and was subsequently named Fabricator-General, though his rival Hal's survival left the succession unresolved — the so-called Binary Succession.

Heresy-era Tech-Priest and ambassador of the loyalist Fabricator-General Zagreus Kane to the Council of Terra. Vethorel worked to legitimise Kane's claim by pressing for the Mechanicum's elevation to Adeptus status, making her central to the political crisis of the Binary Succession.

Space Wolves Jarl of the 13th Great Company (Dekk-Tra) during the Horus Heresy. Bulveye led his warriors into the depths of Tizca during the Burning of Prospero, pursuing a sorcerer through a warp portal — an act that stranded the entire company in the Eye of Terror for ten millennia until their recovery during the 13th Black Crusade.

Former Death Guard Astartes and Techmarine serving as second-in-command in Endryd Haar's Blackshield warband, the Fangs of the Emperor. In the trilogy's finale, Vahn betrays Haar — bargaining with their Sons of Horus captors while his commander is left imprisoned and dying in chains.

Iron Warriors Warsmith and sole survivor aboard a wrecked transport in the aftermath of the Battle of Tallarn. Stranded on the virus-scarred surface and slowly dying, Koparnos pursues both survival and vengeance by salvaging the wreck of a downed Warlord-class Titan.
